[[ja:%E7%9B%8A%E5%AD%90%E7%A5%87%E5%9C%92%E7%A5%AD]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]][[Category: tier 6]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Mashiko Gion Festival]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Mashiko Gion Festival]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Mashiko Gion Festival]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Mashiko Gion Festival]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Mashiko Gion Festival]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11 deprecated open]]<!--Mashiko Gion Festival--> [[Category:Mashiko Gion Festival]][[Category:Mashiko Gion Festival]][[Category:Mashiko Gion Festival]] [[Category:11]][[en:Mashiko Gion Festival]][[Category:11]] [[en:Mashiko Gion Festival]][[en:Mashiko Gion Festival]] '''Mashiko Gion Festival''' (益子祇園祭) is a festival held annually from {{ill|July 23|ja|7月23日}} to {{ill|July 25|ja|7月25日}} in {{ill|Mashiko, Tochigi|ja|益子町}}, Tochigi Prefecture. It is the festival of {{ill|Yasaka Shrine (Mashiko)|ja|八坂神社}}, which is a subordinate shrine within the grounds of {{ill|Kashima Shrine (Mashiko)|ja|鹿島神社 (益子町)}}<ref>[https://www.town.mashiko.lg.jp/sp/page/page001983.html "Mashiko Sekai Heritage" No.10｜Yasaka Shrine - Mashiko Town Official Website]</ref><ref name="MashikoJouhouKyoku2012_07_24">{{Cite web | url= http://mashikojk.blog.fc2.com/blog-entry-19.html | title= Mashiko's Summer - Gion Festival Departure Ceremony & Handheld Fireworks Dedication | website= Mashiko Information Bureau | date= 2012-7-24 | accessdate=2024-8-1}}</ref><ref name="TouKo_MashikoGionMaturi_2023">{{Cite instagram|user=tokomashiko |author=Tōko / Mashiko Pottery Gallery |postid=CvOdm-FPlD_ |title=Reiwa 5th Year Mashiko Gion Festival was held from July 23 to 25... |date=2023-07-28|access-date=2024-07-26}}</ref><ref name="Mashiko_no_Omatsuri">[http://www.mashiko-kankou.org/culture/traditional_culture.shtml Mashiko Culture - Traditional Culture | Mashiko Tourism Association]</ref><ref name="MashikoGionMatsuri_MashikoSekenIsan_054">{{Cite web | url= https://www.town.mashiko.lg.jp/sp/page/page002973.html | title= Mashiko Sekai Heritage No.54｜Yasaka Shrine Gion Festival (Mashiko Gion Festival / Tenno Festival) | website= Mashiko Town Official Website | date= | accessdate=2024-7-30}}</ref>.
One of the most famous events is the '''"Omiki Choudai-shiki"''' (御神酒頂戴式), a ritual for passing the town's duty, which is considered one of the '''"Three Great Peculiar Festivals of Kanto"''' and is held on the middle day, {{ill|July 24|ja|7月24日}}<ref name=MashikoGionMatsuri_MashikoSekenIsan_054/><ref name=TouKo_MashikoGionMaturi_2023/>{{sfn|Kashiwamura Yūji, Festivals of Tochigi|2012|p=106-108}}.
== Overview == The '''Yasaka Shrine''' in Mashiko is a subordinate shrine of {{ill|Kashima Shrine (Mashiko)|ja|鹿島神社 (益子町)}}<ref name=MashikoGionMatsuri_MashikoSekenIsan_054/>. Every year from '''July 23 to July 25''', the Gion Festival ('''formerly called Tenno Festival''') is held<ref name=MashikoJouhouKyoku2012_07_24/><ref name=MashikoGionMatsuri_MashikoSekenIsan_054/>.
The festival is also known as '''"Yasaka Shrine Gion Festival"'''{{sfn|Kashiwamura Yūji, Festivals of Tochigi|2012|p=106-108}}.
The central town districts of Mashiko, such as '''Shinmachi, Tamachi, Uchimachi, Jōnai, and Dōsodo''', take turns being the hosting town. These five towns, along with '''Hanawa District''', participate with '''festival floats''' that parade through Mashiko’s main streets in a grand celebration{{sfn|Mashiko Exploration, Kuramoto Hidekiyo|1992|p=72}}<ref name=MashikoJouhouKyoku2012_07_24/>.
Many participants are not just original Mashiko residents but also '''potters who have moved to Mashiko''', including '''Takauchi Hideyuki''' and '''Susukida Kōji'''{{sfn|Mashiko Exploration, Kuramoto Hidekiyo|1992|p=72-74}}{{sfn|Shimotsuke Newspaper|1984|p=94-95}}<ref name=SusukidaKouji_no_DeshiTachi>{{Cite instagram|user=ito137.3life |author=ito_susukida |postid=BbiaHMtg5kk |title=Father and his seven apprentices... |date=2017-11-18 |access-date=2023-07-19}}</ref>.
== Origin == Around '''Hōei 2 (1705)''', an '''epidemic''' broke out in Mashiko. To pray for its eradication, the '''Gion belief''' (祇園信仰) originating from Kyoto in the Heian period was introduced{{sfn|Michikake Vol.5, Mashiko Town|2015|p=14}}, and people began '''worshiping Gozu Tennō''', the deity of plagues<ref name=MashikoJouhouKyoku2012_07_24/><ref name=TouKo_MashikoGionMaturi_2023/>{{sfn|Kashiwamura Yūji, Festivals of Tochigi|2012|p=106-108}}.
---

Many participants are not just original Mashiko residents but also '''potters who have moved to Mashiko''', including '''Takauchi Hideyuki''' and '''Susukida Kōji'''{{sfn|Mashiko Exploration, Kuramoto Hidekiyo|1992|p=72-74}}{{sfn|Shimotsuke Newspaper|1984|p=94-95}}<ref name=SusukidaKouji_no_DeshiTachi>{{Cite instagram|user=ito137.3life |author=ito_susukida |postid=BbiaHMtg5kk |title=Father and his seven apprentices... |date=2017-11-18 |access-date=2023-07-19}}</ref>.

To pray for its eradication, the '''Gion belief''' (祇園信仰) originating from Kyoto in the Heian period was introduced{{sfn|Michikake Vol.5, Mashiko Town|2015|p=14}}, and people began '''worshiping Gozu Tennō''', the deity of plagues<ref name=MashikoJouhouKyoku2012_07_24/><ref name=TouKo_MashikoGionMaturi_2023/>{{sfn|Kashiwamura Yūji, Festivals of Tochigi|2012|p=106-108}}.
---

[[ja:%E7%9B%8A%E5%AD%90%E7%A5%87%E5%9C%92%E7%A5%AD]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]][[Category: tier 6]][[Category:11]][[sn:Mashiko Gion Festival]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Mashiko Gion Festival]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Mashiko Gion Festival]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Mashiko Gion Festival]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Mashiko Gion Festival]][[Category:11]] <!--Mashiko Gion Festival--> [[Category:Mashiko Gion Festival]][[Category:Mashiko Gion Festival]][[Category:Mashiko Gion Festival]] [[Category:11]][[en:Mashiko Gion Festival]][[Category:11]] [[en:Mashiko Gion Festival]][[en:Mashiko Gion Festival]] '''Mashiko Gion Festival''' (益子祇園祭) is a festival held annually from {{ill|July 23|ja|7月23日}} to {{ill|July 25|ja|7月25日}} in {{ill|Mashiko, Tochigi|ja|益子町}}, Tochigi Prefecture. It is the festival of {{ill|Yasaka Shrine (Mashiko)|ja|八坂神社}}, which is a subordinate shrine within the grounds of {{ill|Kashima Shrine (Mashiko)|ja|鹿島神社 (益子町)}}<ref>[https://www.town.mashiko.lg.jp/sp/page/page001983.html "Mashiko Sekai Heritage" No.10｜Yasaka Shrine - Mashiko Town Official Website]</ref><ref name="MashikoJouhouKyoku2012_07_24">{{Cite web | url= http://mashikojk.blog.fc2.com/blog-entry-19.html | title= Mashiko's Summer - Gion Festival Departure Ceremony & Handheld Fireworks Dedication | website= Mashiko Information Bureau | date= 2012-7-24 | accessdate=2024-8-1}}</ref><ref name="TouKo_MashikoGionMaturi_2023">{{Cite instagram|user=tokomashiko |author=Tōko / Mashiko Pottery Gallery |postid=CvOdm-FPlD_ |title=Reiwa 5th Year Mashiko Gion Festival was held from July 23 to 25... |date=2023-07-28|access-date=2024-07-26}}</ref><ref name="Mashiko_no_Omatsuri">[http://www.mashiko-kankou.org/culture/traditional_culture.shtml Mashiko Culture - Traditional Culture | Mashiko Tourism Association]</ref><ref name="MashikoGionMatsuri_MashikoSekenIsan_054">{{Cite web | url= https://www.town.mashiko.lg.jp/sp/page/page002973.html | title= Mashiko Sekai Heritage No.54｜Yasaka Shrine Gion Festival (Mashiko Gion Festival / Tenno Festival) | website= Mashiko Town Official Website | date= | accessdate=2024-7-30}}</ref>.
One of the most famous events is the '''"Omiki Choudai-shiki"''' (御神酒頂戴式), a ritual for passing the town's duty, which is considered one of the '''"Three Great Peculiar Festivals of Kanto"''' and is held on the middle day, {{ill|July 24|ja|7月24日}}<ref name=MashikoGionMatsuri_MashikoSekenIsan_054/><ref name=TouKo_MashikoGionMaturi_2023/>{{sfn|Kashiwamura Yūji, Festivals of Tochigi|2012|p=106-108}}.
== Overview == The '''Yasaka Shrine''' in Mashiko is a subordinate shrine of {{ill|Kashima Shrine (Mashiko)|ja|鹿島神社 (益子町)}}<ref name=MashikoGionMatsuri_MashikoSekenIsan_054/>. Every year from '''July 23 to July 25''', the Gion Festival ('''formerly called Tenno Festival''') is held<ref name=MashikoJouhouKyoku2012_07_24/><ref name=MashikoGionMatsuri_MashikoSekenIsan_054/>.
The festival is also known as '''"Yasaka Shrine Gion Festival"'''{{sfn|Kashiwamura Yūji, Festivals of Tochigi|2012|p=106-108}}.
The central town districts of Mashiko, such as '''Shinmachi, Tamachi, Uchimachi, Jōnai, and Dōsodo''', take turns being the hosting town. These five towns, along with '''Hanawa District''', participate with '''festival floats''' that parade through Mashiko’s main streets in a grand celebration{{sfn|Mashiko Exploration, Kuramoto Hidekiyo|1992|p=72}}<ref name=MashikoJouhouKyoku2012_07_24/>.
Many participants are not just original Mashiko residents but also '''potters who have moved to Mashiko''', including '''Takauchi Hideyuki''' and '''Susukida Kōji'''{{sfn|Mashiko Exploration, Kuramoto Hidekiyo|1992|p=72-74}}{{sfn|Shimotsuke Newspaper|1984|p=94-95}}<ref name=SusukidaKouji_no_DeshiTachi>{{Cite instagram|user=ito137.3life |author=ito_susukida |postid=BbiaHMtg5kk |title=Father and his seven apprentices... |date=2017-11-18 |access-date=2023-07-19}}</ref>.
== Origin == Around '''Hōei 2 (1705)''', an '''epidemic''' broke out in Mashiko. To pray for its eradication, the '''Gion belief''' (祇園信仰) originating from Kyoto in the Heian period was introduced{{sfn|Michikake Vol.5, Mashiko Town|2015|p=14}}, and people began '''worshiping Gozu Tennō''', the deity of plagues<ref name=MashikoJouhouKyoku2012_07_24/><ref name=TouKo_MashikoGionMaturi_2023/>{{sfn|Kashiwamura Yūji, Festivals of Tochigi|2012|p=106-108}}.
---

These five towns, along with '''Hanawa District''', participate with '''festival floats''' that parade through Mashiko’s main streets in a grand celebration{{sfn|Mashiko Exploration, Kuramoto Hidekiyo|1992|p=72}}<ref name=MashikoJouhouKyoku2012_07_24/>.
Many participants are not just original Mashiko residents but also '''potters who have moved to Mashiko''', including '''Takauchi Hideyuki''' and '''Susukida Kōji'''{{sfn|Mashiko Exploration, Kuramoto Hidekiyo|1992|p=72-74}}{{sfn|Shimotsuke Newspaper|1984|p=94-95}}<ref name=SusukidaKouji_no_DeshiTachi>{{Cite instagram|user=ito137.3life |author=ito_susukida |postid=BbiaHMtg5kk |title=Father and his seven apprentices... |date=2017-11-18 |access-date=2023-07-19}}</ref>.
== Origin == Around '''Hōei 2 (1705)''', an '''epidemic''' broke out in Mashiko. To pray for its eradication, the '''Gion belief''' (祇園信仰) originating from Kyoto in the Heian period was introduced{{sfn|Michikake Vol.5, Mashiko Town|2015|p=14}}, and people began '''worshiping Gozu Tennō''', the deity of plagues<ref name=MashikoJouhouKyoku2012_07_24/><ref name=TouKo_MashikoGionMaturi_2023/>{{sfn|Kashiwamura Yūji, Festivals of Tochigi|2012|p=106-108}}.
---

<!--Mashiko Gion Festival-->
'''Mashiko Gion Festival''' (益子祇園祭) is a festival held annually from {{ill|July 23|ja|7月23日|12=draft}} to {{ill|July 25|ja|7月25日|12=draft}} in {{ill|Mashiko, Tochigi|ja|益子町|12=draft}}, Tochigi Prefecture. It is the festival of {{ill|Yasaka Shrine (Mashiko)|ja|八坂神社|12=draft}}, which is a subordinate shrine within the grounds of {{ill|Kashima Shrine (Mashiko)|ja|鹿島神社 (益子町)|12=draft}}<ref>[https://www.town.mashiko.lg.jp/sp/page/page001983.html "Mashiko Sekai Heritage" No.10｜Yasaka Shrine - Mashiko Town Official Website]</ref><ref name="MashikoJouhouKyoku2012_07_24">{{Cite web | url= http://mashikojk.blog.fc2.com/blog-entry-19.html | title= Mashiko's Summer - Gion Festival Departure Ceremony & Handheld Fireworks Dedication | website= Mashiko Information Bureau | date= 2012-7-24 | accessdate=2024-8-1}}</ref><ref name="TouKo_MashikoGionMaturi_2023">{{Cite instagram|user=tokomashiko |author=Tōko / Mashiko Pottery Gallery |postid=CvOdm-FPlD_ |title=Reiwa 5th Year Mashiko Gion Festival was held from July 23 to 25... |date=2023-07-28|access-date=2024-07-26}}</ref><ref name="Mashiko_no_Omatsuri">[http://www.mashiko-kankou.org/culture/traditional_culture.shtml Mashiko Culture - Traditional Culture | Mashiko Tourism Association]</ref><ref name="MashikoGionMatsuri_MashikoSekenIsan_054">{{Cite web | url= https://www.town.mashiko.lg.jp/sp/page/page002973.html | title= Mashiko Sekai Heritage No.54｜Yasaka Shrine Gion Festival (Mashiko Gion Festival / Tenno Festival) | website= Mashiko Town Official Website | date= | accessdate=2024-7-30}}</ref>.
One of the most famous events is the '''"Omiki Choudai-shiki"''' (御神酒頂戴式), a ritual for passing the town's duty, which is considered one of the '''"Three Great Peculiar Festivals of Kanto"''' and is held on the middle day, {{ill|July 24|ja|7月24日|12=draft}}<ref name=MashikoGionMatsuri_MashikoSekenIsan_054/><ref name=TouKo_MashikoGionMaturi_2023/>{{sfn|Kashiwamura Yūji, Festivals of Tochigi|2012|p=106-108}}.
== Overview == The '''Yasaka Shrine''' in Mashiko is a subordinate shrine of {{ill|Kashima Shrine (Mashiko)|ja|鹿島神社 (益子町)|12=draft}}<ref name=MashikoGionMatsuri_MashikoSekenIsan_054/>. Every year from '''July 23 to July 25''', the Gion Festival ('''formerly called Tenno Festival''') is held<ref name=MashikoJouhouKyoku2012_07_24/><ref name=MashikoGionMatsuri_MashikoSekenIsan_054/>.
The festival is also known as '''"Yasaka Shrine Gion Festival"'''{{sfn|Kashiwamura Yūji, Festivals of Tochigi|2012|p=106-108}}.
The central town districts of Mashiko, such as '''Shinmachi, Tamachi, Uchimachi, Jōnai, and Dōsodo''', take turns being the hosting town. These five towns, along with '''Hanawa District''', participate with '''festival floats''' that parade through Mashiko’s main streets in a grand celebration{{sfn|Mashiko Exploration, Kuramoto Hidekiyo|1992|p=72}}<ref name=MashikoJouhouKyoku2012_07_24/>.
Many participants are not just original Mashiko residents but also '''potters who have moved to Mashiko''', including '''Takauchi Hideyuki''' and '''Susukida Kōji'''{{sfn|Mashiko Exploration, Kuramoto Hidekiyo|1992|p=72-74}}{{sfn|Shimotsuke Newspaper|1984|p=94-95}}<ref name=SusukidaKouji_no_DeshiTachi>{{Cite instagram|user=ito137.3life |author=ito_susukida |postid=BbiaHMtg5kk |title=Father and his seven apprentices... |date=2017-11-18 |access-date=2023-07-19}}</ref>.
== Origin == Around '''Hōei 2 (1705)''', an '''epidemic''' broke out in Mashiko. To pray for its eradication, the '''Gion belief''' (祇園信仰) originating from Kyoto in the Heian period was introduced{{sfn|Michikake Vol.5, Mashiko Town|2015|p=14}}, and people began '''worshiping Gozu Tennō''', the deity of plagues<ref name=MashikoJouhouKyoku2012_07_24/><ref name=TouKo_MashikoGionMaturi_2023/>{{sfn|Kashiwamura Yūji, Festivals of Tochigi|2012|p=106-108}}.
---

revision.text:
'''Mashiko Gion Festival''' (益子祇園祭) is a festival held annually from {{ill|July 23|ja|7月23日|12=draft|12=draft}} to {{ill|July 25|ja|7月25日|12=draft|12=draft}} in {{ill|Mashiko, Tochigi|ja|益子町|12=draft|12=draft}}, Tochigi Prefecture. It is the festival of {{ill|Yasaka Shrine (Mashiko)|ja|八坂神社|12=draft|12=draft}}, which is a subordinate shrine within the grounds of {{ill|Kashima Shrine (Mashiko)|ja|鹿島神社 (益子町)|12=draft|12=draft}}<ref>[https://www.town.mashiko.lg.jp/sp/page/page001983.html "Mashiko Sekai Heritage" No.10｜Yasaka Shrine - Mashiko Town Official Website]</ref><ref name="MashikoJouhouKyoku2012_07_24">{{Cite web | url= http://mashikojk.blog.fc2.com/blog-entry-19.html | title= Mashiko's Summer - Gion Festival Departure Ceremony & Handheld Fireworks Dedication | website= Mashiko Information Bureau | date= 2012-7-24 | accessdate=2024-8-1}}</ref><ref name="TouKo_MashikoGionMaturi_2023">{{Cite instagram|user=tokomashiko |author=Tōko / Mashiko Pottery Gallery |postid=CvOdm-FPlD_ |title=Reiwa 5th Year Mashiko Gion Festival was held from July 23 to 25... |date=2023-07-28|access-date=2024-07-26}}</ref><ref name="Mashiko_no_Omatsuri">[http://www.mashiko-kankou.org/culture/traditional_culture.shtml Mashiko Culture - Traditional Culture | Mashiko Tourism Association]</ref><ref name="MashikoGionMatsuri_MashikoSekenIsan_054">{{Cite web | url= https://www.town.mashiko.lg.jp/sp/page/page002973.html | title= Mashiko Sekai Heritage No.54｜Yasaka Shrine Gion Festival (Mashiko Gion Festival / Tenno Festival) | website= Mashiko Town Official Website | date= | accessdate=2024-7-30}}</ref>.
One of the most famous events is the '''"Omiki Choudai-shiki"''' (御神酒頂戴式), a ritual for passing the town's duty, which is considered one of the '''"Three Great Peculiar Festivals of Kanto"''' and is held on the middle day, {{ill|July 24|ja|7月24日|12=draft|12=draft}}<ref name=MashikoGionMatsuri_MashikoSekenIsan_054/><ref name=TouKo_MashikoGionMaturi_2023/>{{sfn|Kashiwamura Yūji, Festivals of Tochigi|2012|p=106-108}}.
== Overview == The '''Yasaka Shrine''' in Mashiko is a subordinate shrine of {{ill|Kashima Shrine (Mashiko)|ja|鹿島神社 (益子町)|12=draft|12=draft}}<ref name=MashikoGionMatsuri_MashikoSekenIsan_054/>. Every year from '''July 23 to July 25''', the Gion Festival ('''formerly called Tenno Festival''') is held<ref name=MashikoJouhouKyoku2012_07_24/><ref name=MashikoGionMatsuri_MashikoSekenIsan_054/>.
The festival is also known as '''"Yasaka Shrine Gion Festival"'''{{sfn|Kashiwamura Yūji, Festivals of Tochigi|2012|p=106-108}}.
The central town districts of Mashiko, such as '''Shinmachi, Tamachi, Uchimachi, Jōnai, and Dōsodo''', take turns being the hosting town. These five towns, along with '''Hanawa District''', participate with '''festival floats''' that parade through Mashiko’s main streets in a grand celebration{{sfn|Mashiko Exploration, Kuramoto Hidekiyo|1992|p=72}}<ref name=MashikoJouhouKyoku2012_07_24/>.
Many participants are not just original Mashiko residents but also '''potters who have moved to Mashiko''', including '''Takauchi Hideyuki''' and '''Susukida Kōji'''{{sfn|Mashiko Exploration, Kuramoto Hidekiyo|1992|p=72-74}}{{sfn|Shimotsuke Newspaper|1984|p=94-95}}<ref name=SusukidaKouji_no_DeshiTachi>{{Cite instagram|user=ito137.3life |author=ito_susukida |postid=BbiaHMtg5kk |title=Father and his seven apprentices... |date=2017-11-18 |access-date=2023-07-19}}</ref>.
== Origin == Around '''Hōei 2 (1705)''', an '''epidemic''' broke out in Mashiko. To pray for its eradication, the '''Gion belief''' (祇園信仰) originating from Kyoto in the Heian period was introduced{{sfn|Michikake Vol.5, Mashiko Town|2015|p=14}}, and people began '''worshiping Gozu Tennō''', the deity of plagues<ref name=MashikoJouhouKyoku2012_07_24/><ref name=TouKo_MashikoGionMaturi_2023/>{{sfn|Kashiwamura Yūji, Festivals of Tochigi|2012|p=106-108}}.
---

The central town districts of Mashiko, such as '''Shinmachi, Tamachi, Uchimachi, Jōnai, and Dōsodo''', take turns being the hosting town. These five towns, along with '''Hanawa District''', participate with '''festival floats''' that parade through Mashiko’s main streets in a grand celebration{{sfn|Mashiko Exploration, Kuramoto Hidekiyo|1992|p=72}}<ref name=MashikoJouhouKyoku2012_07_24/>.
Many participants are not just original Mashiko residents but also '''potters who have moved to Mashiko''', including '''Takauchi Hideyuki''' and '''Susukida Kōji'''{{sfn|Mashiko Exploration, Kuramoto Hidekiyo|1992|p=72-74}}{{sfn|Shimotsuke Newspaper|1984|p=94-95}}<ref name=SusukidaKouji_no_DeshiTachi>{{Cite instagram|user=ito137.3life |author=ito_susukida |postid=BbiaHMtg5kk |title=Father and his seven apprentices... |date=2017-11-18 |access-date=2023-07-19}}</ref>.
== Origin == Around '''Hōei 2 (1705)''', an '''epidemic''' broke out in Mashiko. To pray for its eradication, the '''Gion belief''' (祇園信仰) originating from Kyoto in the Heian period was introduced{{sfn|Michikake Vol.5, Mashiko Town|2015|p=14}}, and people began '''worshiping Gozu Tennō''', the deity of plagues<ref name=MashikoJouhouKyoku2012_07_24/><ref name=TouKo_MashikoGionMaturi_2023/>{{sfn|Kashiwamura Yūji, Festivals of Tochigi|2012|p=106-108}}.
---
=== '''Category & External Links'''=== * [http://www.mashiko-kankou.org/culture/traditional_culture.shtml Mashiko Culture | Traditional Culture | Gion Festival] * '''Mashiko Tourism Association''' * [https://www.town.mashiko.lg.jp/sp/page/page002973.html Mashiko Sekai Heritage No.54 | Yasaka Shrine Gion Festival] * '''Mashiko Town Official Website''' * [https://tabi-mag.jp/mashiko-gion/ Mashiko Gion Festival] * '''Nippon Tabi Magazine'''
{{draft categories|
}}
{{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Mashiko Gion Festival]] [[Category:Festivals in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:Gion Festivals & Tennō Festivals]] [[Category:Mashiko Town]] [[Category:History of Mashiko Town]] [[Category:New]] }}
[[ja:%E7%9B%8A%E5%AD%90%E7%A5%87%E5%9C%92%E7%A5%AD]] [[sn:Mashiko Gion Festival]] [[en:Mashiko Gion Festival]]
<!--Mashiko Gion Festival-->

'''Mashiko Gion Festival''' (益子祇園祭) is a festival held annually from {{ill|July 23|ja|7月23日}} to {{ill|July 25|ja|7月25日}} in {{ill|Mashiko, Tochigi|ja|益子町}}, Tochigi Prefecture. It is the festival of {{ill|Yasaka Shrine (Mashiko)|ja|八坂神社}}, which is a subordinate shrine within the grounds of {{ill|Kashima Shrine (Mashiko)|ja|鹿島神社 (益子町)}}<ref>[https://www.town.mashiko.lg.jp/sp/page/page001983.html "Mashiko Sekai Heritage" No.10｜Yasaka Shrine - Mashiko Town Official Website]</ref><ref name="MashikoJouhouKyoku2012_07_24">{{Cite web | url= http://mashikojk.blog.fc2.com/blog-entry-19.html | title= Mashiko's Summer - Gion Festival Departure Ceremony & Handheld Fireworks Dedication | website= Mashiko Information Bureau | date= 2012-7-24 | accessdate=2024-8-1}}</ref><ref name="TouKo_MashikoGionMaturi_2023">{{Cite instagram|user=tokomashiko |author=Tōko / Mashiko Pottery Gallery |postid=CvOdm-FPlD_ |title=Reiwa 5th Year Mashiko Gion Festival was held from July 23 to 25... |date=2023-07-28|access-date=2024-07-26}}</ref><ref name="Mashiko_no_Omatsuri">[http://www.mashiko-kankou.org/culture/traditional_culture.shtml Mashiko Culture - Traditional Culture | Mashiko Tourism Association]</ref><ref name="MashikoGionMatsuri_MashikoSekenIsan_054">{{Cite web | url= https://www.town.mashiko.lg.jp/sp/page/page002973.html | title= Mashiko Sekai Heritage No.54｜Yasaka Shrine Gion Festival (Mashiko Gion Festival / Tenno Festival) | website= Mashiko Town Official Website | date= | accessdate=2024-7-30}}</ref>.
One of the most famous events is the '''"Omiki Choudai-shiki"''' (御神酒頂戴式), a ritual for passing the town's duty, which is considered one of the '''"Three Great Peculiar Festivals of Kanto"''' and is held on the middle day, {{ill|July 24|ja|7月24日}}<ref name=MashikoGionMatsuri_MashikoSekenIsan_054/><ref name=TouKo_MashikoGionMaturi_2023/>{{sfn|Kashiwamura Yūji, Festivals of Tochigi|2012|p=106-108}}.
== Overview == The '''Yasaka Shrine''' in Mashiko is a subordinate shrine of {{ill|Kashima Shrine (Mashiko)|ja|鹿島神社 (益子町)}}<ref name=MashikoGionMatsuri_MashikoSekenIsan_054/>. Every year from '''July 23 to July 25''', the Gion Festival ('''formerly called Tenno Festival''') is held<ref name=MashikoJouhouKyoku2012_07_24/><ref name=MashikoGionMatsuri_MashikoSekenIsan_054/>.
The festival is also known as '''"Yasaka Shrine Gion Festival"'''{{sfn|Kashiwamura Yūji, Festivals of Tochigi|2012|p=106-108}}.
The central town districts of Mashiko, such as '''Shinmachi, Tamachi, Uchimachi, Jōnai, and Dōsodo''', take turns being the hosting town. These five towns, along with '''Hanawa District''', participate with '''festival floats''' that parade through Mashiko’s main streets in a grand celebration{{sfn|Mashiko Exploration, Kuramoto Hidekiyo|1992|p=72}}<ref name=MashikoJouhouKyoku2012_07_24/>.
Many participants are not just original Mashiko residents but also '''potters who have moved to Mashiko''', including '''Takauchi Hideyuki''' and '''Susukida Kōji'''{{sfn|Mashiko Exploration, Kuramoto Hidekiyo|1992|p=72-74}}{{sfn|Shimotsuke Newspaper|1984|p=94-95}}<ref name=SusukidaKouji_no_DeshiTachi>{{Cite instagram|user=ito137.3life |author=ito_susukida |postid=BbiaHMtg5kk |title=Father and his seven apprentices... |date=2017-11-18 |access-date=2023-07-19}}</ref>.
== Origin == Around '''Hōei 2 (1705)''', an '''epidemic''' broke out in Mashiko. To pray for its eradication, the '''Gion belief''' (祇園信仰) originating from Kyoto in the Heian period was introduced{{sfn|Michikake Vol.5, Mashiko Town|2015|p=14}}, and people began '''worshiping Gozu Tennō''', the deity of plagues<ref name=MashikoJouhouKyoku2012_07_24/><ref name=TouKo_MashikoGionMaturi_2023/>{{sfn|Kashiwamura Yūji, Festivals of Tochigi|2012|p=106-108}}.
---
=== '''Category & External Links'''=== * [http://www.mashiko-kankou.org/culture/traditional_culture.shtml Mashiko Culture | Traditional Culture | Gion Festival] * '''Mashiko Tourism Association''' * [https://www.town.mashiko.lg.jp/sp/page/page002973.html Mashiko Sekai Heritage No.54 | Yasaka Shrine Gion Festival] * '''Mashiko Town Official Website''' * [https://tabi-mag.jp/mashiko-gion/ Mashiko Gion Festival] * '''Nippon Tabi Magazine'''

revision.comment: 142 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:益子祇園祭 up to rev 102298556
revision.text:
{{Pathnav|栃木県|益子町|益子祇園祭|hide = 1|frame=1}} {{イベントインフォメーション |イベント名称 = 益子祇園祭 |英文表記 = |画像 = |画像サイズ = |画像説明 = |種類 = 祇園祭（山王祭） |通称 =（益子訛りの方言として）ギョン祭り{{sfn|益子探訪,倉本秀清|1992|p=72}} |正式名称 = |旧名称 = |開催時期 = [[7月23日]]、[[7月24日|24日]]、[[7月25日|25日]] |初回開催 = |最終開催 = |開催時間 |会場 = [[栃木県]][[益子町]]町内の各所。
城内町、田町、道祖土町などの各参加町の主要道路。
栃木県益子町鹿島神社前。 |主催 = |共催 = |後援 = |協賛 = |企画制作 = |協力 = |運営 = |プロデューサー = |出展数 = |来場者数 = |会場アクセス名 = |最寄駅 =[[益子駅]] |直通バス = |駐車場 = |URL = |特記事項 = }} '''益子祇園祭'''（ましこぎおんまつり）は、[[栃木県]][[芳賀郡]][[益子町]]で毎年[[7月23日]]から[[7月25日]]の3日間、益子にある[[鹿島神社 (益子町)|鹿島神社]]の境内末社である八坂神社[https://www.town.mashiko.lg.jp/sp/page/page001983.html 「ましこ世間遺産」認定No.10｜八坂神社-益子町公式ホームページ]の祭礼として行われる祭りである{{Cite web | url= http://mashikojk.blog.fc2.com/blog-entry-19.html | title= 益子の夏～祇園祭　-出御祭・手筒花火奉納- | website= 益子情報局 | date= 2012-7-24 | accessdate=2024-8-1}}{{Cite instagram|user=tokomashiko |author=陶庫／益子焼 陶器ギャラリー |postid=CvOdm-FPlD_ |title=令和五年度益子祇園祭が7月23日～25日に斎行されました。……… |date=2023-07-28|access-date=2024-07-26}}[http://www.mashiko-kankou.org/culture/traditional_culture.shtml 益子の文化 伝統文化｜益子町観光協会]{{Cite web | url= https://www.town.mashiko.lg.jp/sp/page/page002973.html | title= ましこ世間遺産 認定No.54｜八坂神社祇園祭（益子祇園祭・天王祭）　町政｜町の紹介 | website= 益子町 公式ホームページ | date= | accessdate=2024-7-30}}。 中日の[[7月24日]]に、「当番町引き継ぎの儀式」である神事の一つとして「関東三大奇祭」の一つとされることもある「'''[[御神酒頂戴式]]'''」が行われることで有名である{{sfn|柏村祐司,栃木の祭り|2012|p=106-108}}。 == 概要 == 益子町にある八坂神社は、同じく益子町にある鹿島神社の境内末社である。そして毎年、7月23日、24日、25日の3日間、祇園祭（過去には天王祭と呼ばれていた）が行われる。 益子の祇園祭は「八坂神社の祇園祭」とも呼ばれている{{sfn|柏村祐司,栃木の祭り|2012|p=106-108}}。 益子の中心部にある氏子地区である新町、田町、内町、城内、道祖土が当番制で当番町を務め、この各5町と塙地区からそれぞれ[[山車]]が参加し、益子町の中心街を練り歩く盛大な祭りである{{sfn|益子探訪,倉本秀清|1992|p=72}}。 もともとの益子町民のみならず、[[高内秀剛]]や{{sfn|益子探訪,倉本秀清|1992|p=72-74}}[[薄田浩司]]など、益子町に移住した[[陶芸家]]の中にも積極的に参加する者もいる{{sfn|下野新聞社|1984|p=94-95}}{{Cite instagram|user=ito137.3life |author=ito_susukida 薄田いと |postid=BbiaHMtg5kk |title=父と父のもとで修行した7名とで… |date=2017-11-18 |access-date=2023-07-19}}。 == 由来 == [[宝永]]2年（[[1705年]]）頃、益子に疫病が流行った際に、平安時代の京都から伝わる天王信仰（[[祇園信仰]]）に基づき{{sfn|ミチカケ 第5号,益子町|2015|p=14}}、疫病退散を祈り疫病を司る[[牛頭天王]]を祭ったことから始まったとされている{{sfn|柏村祐司,栃木の祭り|2012|p=106-108}}。 == 昔の祭事日程と内容 == 昔は[[陰暦]]に基づいて祭事が行われていた[{{NDLDC|815437/17}} 『下野神社沿革誌 巻之6』風山広雄 編「益子町」「益子町大字益子鎮座」「村社 鹿島神社」十五 - 十六 - 国立国会図書館デジタルコレクション]　2023年9月23日閲覧。 陰暦の6月15日に仮屋への神輿の橋渡しが行われる。 そして陰暦6月23日から大祭が挙行される。 陰暦6月23日には[[氏子]]一同に神酒が賜る。当番引き継ぎには、当時の一年の日数に合わせた三升六合入りの大杯を用いて神酒を賜った。これが現在の「[[御神酒頂戴式]]」へと繋がっている。 陰暦6月24日には神輿の入御が行われ、昔は警衛士、大旗、騎馬乗馬の鎧武者、神主、靴持ち、道具持ち、村役人などによる行列式が行われた。 そして陰暦6月25日には、当時の益子村の領主であった[[黒羽藩]]主・[[大関氏]]へ、神主からお祓い献上の儀式が行われた。 == 日程と各種行事 == === あじさい祭り === 6月末に、益子町の｢あじさい公園｣内にある｢太平神社｣で{{Cite web | url= http://blog.mashiko-kankou.org/staff/?p=22545 | title= あじさい祭り（2024年6月24日～7月25日まで）｜お知らせ | website= 益子観光協会 | date= 2024-6-1 | accessdate=2024-8-1}}、益子祇園祭シーズンの始まりを告げる｢あじさい祭り｣が開かれる{{sfn|ミチカケ 第5号,益子町|2015|p=8}}。 [[稚児#祭りにおける稚児|稚児]]が[[巫女]]に扮し[[紫陽花]]を献花する｢献花祭｣と 、境内に作られた｢茅の輪｣をくぐり厄を祓う｢[[大祓#茅の輪くぐり|茅の輪くぐり]]・[[大祓|大祓式]]｣が執り行われる。 また境内と参道に飾られる[[提灯]]は、｢益子祇園祭｣が終わる7月25日まで灯し続けられる。 === 手筒花火：下野手筒会 === [[2005年]]（平成17年）から採用され「下野新聞」2005年（平成17年）7月22日付 11面「手筒花火を披露」「24日に益子祇園祭」「本場・愛知で習ってきました」「自営業者の会が実演」「下野新聞」2006年（平成18年）7月22日付 31面「手筒花火を今年も披露」「祇園祭の24日」「下野新聞」2006年（平成18年）7月26日付 26面「火の粉浴びながら」「益子の下野手筒会」「勇壮な打ち上げ披露」、益子町在住の造形家・KINTA[http://www14.plala.or.jp/KINTA-STUDIO/info/info.html KINTA STUDIO【ご案内】][http://www14.plala.or.jp/KINTA-STUDIO/about/profile.html KINTA／キンタ【プロフィール】]{{Instagram|kinta_studio0105|永田茂夫 作家名 KINTA}}{{sfn|枻出版社,焼き物の里を訪ねて益子・笠間|2009|p=86-89}}を中心として結成された「下野手筒会」により[http://www14.plala.or.jp/KINTA-STUDIO/about/mashiko/tedutsu-kai02.html 下野手筒会について][http://www14.plala.or.jp/KINTA-STUDIO/about/mashiko/tedutsu-kai.html 下野手筒会【しもつけてづつかい】]、益子祇園祭の初日となる[[7月23日]]の夜{{Efn|出典資料の通り、当初は益子祇園祭の2日目となる[[7月24日]]に披露されていた。}}に｢[[手筒花火]]｣が打ち上げられていた{{sfn|柏村祐司,栃木の祭り|2012|p=106-108}}{{Cite web|和書 | url= https://www.shimotsuke.co.jp/articles/-/767424 | title= 雨のように降り注ぐ火の粉に歓声　益子祇園祭で復活の手筒花火 | website= 下野新聞「SOON」 | date=2023-7-24 | accessdate=2023-7-26}}。 その後、[[2024年]]（令和6年）より、益子祇園祭の一環として、祭開催直前の土曜日に披露されることになった{{Cite web | url= http://blog.mashiko-kankou.org/staff/?p=22605 | title= 手筒花火（2024年7月20日開催）｜お知らせ | website= 益子観光協会 | date= 2024-6-14 | accessdate=2024-8-1}}{{Cite web | url= https://www.shimotsuke.co.jp/articles/-/927973 | title= 空に迫力満点の火柱を　地元有志が２０日夜に手筒花火を披露へ　益子 | website= 下野新聞 SOON | date= 2024-7-18 | accessdate=2024-8-1}}{{Cite web | url= https://kitakan-navi.jp/archives/209416 | title= 栃木県益子町で7月20日に手筒花火 迫力満点！高さ8mの火柱が上がる“炎の祭典”を間近で | website= きたかんナビ | date= 2024-7-19 | accessdate=2024-8-1}}{{Cite web | url= https://www.shimotsuke.co.jp/articles/-/929373 | title= 夜空を赤く、熱く照らす炎　益子で「手筒花火」　８メートルの火柱、観客魅了 | website= 下野新聞 SOON | date= 2024-7-20 | accessdate=2024-8-1}}。 === 益子祇園祭 === 日付は曜日とは関係無く、日程を変更すること無く、毎年7月23日、24日、25日の日程で開催される。 1日目となる[[7月23日]]、八坂神社から御分霊された御霊を八坂神社の本社神輿に乗せて担ぎ {{Cite instagram|user=onaidashigumi |author=城内山車組 |postid=CvBsiHeJj9d |title=令和5年 益子祇園祭 初日　始まりました！……… |date=2023-07-23|access-date=2024-07-26}}、[[天狗]]の面を被った[[猿田彦]]により先導され、その年の当番となった地区に設置された「御仮屋」に配置する「神輿の渡御：出御祭」から始まる{{Cite instagram|user=tokomashiko |author=陶庫／益子焼 陶器ギャラリー |postid=CvBtqMwPzH7 |title=御仮屋が陶庫にでき、神様がいらっしゃいました。……… |date=2023-07-23|access-date=2024-07-26}}{{sfn|柏村祐司,栃木の祭り|2012|p=106-108}}{{Cite instagram|user=kashima1115 |author=益子 鹿島神社【公式】 |postid=CvG9cOGsTsg |title=八坂神社 神輿御渡……… |date=2023-07-25|access-date=2024-07-28}}。 そして益子町内の氏子地区である5地区：新町{{Cite instagram|user=kashima1115 |author=益子 鹿島神社【公式】 |postid=CvIBKZvgI7L |title=新町 御上覧……… |date=2023-07-26|access-date=2024-07-30}}・田町{{Cite instagram|user=kashima1115 |author=益子 鹿島神社【公式】 |postid=CvIAwRcsZgJ |title=田町 御上覧……… |date=2023-07-26|access-date=2024-07-30}}・内町{{Cite instagram|user=kashima1115 |author=益子 鹿島神社【公式】 |postid=CvIAG10O5UQ |title=内町 御上覧……… |date=2023-07-26|access-date=2024-07-30}}・城内{{Cite instagram|user=kashima1115 |author=益子 鹿島神社【公式】 |postid=CvH_FTdLERS |title=城内 御上覧……… |date=2023-07-26|access-date=2024-07-30}}・道祖土{{Cite instagram|user=kashima1115 |author=益子 鹿島神社【公式】 |postid=CvIB9fOMp3k |title=道祖土 御上覧……… |date=2023-07-26|access-date=2024-07-30}}と、田野地区の山本を入れる6地区の[[山車]]が「付け祭り」として{{sfn|柏村祐司,栃木の祭り|2012|p=106-108}}益子町内を巡行し、益子の町中を練り歩く{{Cite instagram|user=onaidashigumi |author=城内山車組 |postid=CvCmWtcywKF |title=令和5年 益子祇園祭 初日　無事運行終了しました……… |date=2023-07-23|access-date=2024-07-26}}{{Cite instagram|user=onaidashigumi |author=城内山車組 |postid=CvFYmiKSV6x |title=令和5年 益子祇園祭 2日目　本日も沢山の方々のご協力で、安全に運行することが出来ました。……… |date=2023-07-24|access-date=2024-07-26}}{{Cite instagram|user=onaidashigumi |author=城内山車組 |postid=CvIQ7ioSJ6f |title=令和5年 益子祇園祭 最終日……… |date=2023-07-26|access-date=2024-07-26}}。これは期間中3日間、他の各種行事と平行しながら続けられる。 ちなみに「御神酒頂戴式」は女人禁制であるが、益子祇園祭の牛頭天王は「女性」と言われているためか{{sfn|柏村祐司,栃木の祭り|2012|p=106-108}}、山車の巡行やお囃子の演奏にも女子や女性が積極的に参加する。そして他の地域の祇園祭のような、山車同士でぶつかり合っての荒々しい揉み合いは行われない{{sfn|柏村祐司,栃木の祭り|2012|p=106-108}}。 2日目となる[[7月24日]]には、[[江戸時代]]から伝わる益子町指定[[無形民俗文化財]]であり、[[栃木県]][[日光市]][[輪王寺]]の「[[輪王寺#年中行事|強飯式]]」を代表とする、栃木県に多く存在する「強飯習俗」のうちでも「本物の[[酒]]」を飲むことを強いる、全国でも稀な「当番引き継ぎ式」となる{{sfn|柏村祐司,栃木の祭り|2012|p=106-108}}ことから関東三大奇祭の一つとされている「[[御神酒頂戴式|八坂神社御神酒頂戴式]]（やさかじんじゃおみきちょうだいしき）」が行われる{{sfn|柏村祐司,栃木の祭り|2012|p=106-108}}[https://www.town.mashiko.lg.jp/cultural_property.php?mode=detail&code=20 八坂神社御神酒頂戴式御神酒頂戴式｜文化財詳細｜益子町の文化財 - 益子町公式ホームページ]{{Cite web|和書 | url= https://mashikosc.wordpress.com/2013/08/01/%E7%A5%87%E5%9C%92%E7%A5%AD%E3%82%92%E6%8C%AF%E3%82%8A%E8%BF%94%E3%81%A3%E3%81%A6%E3%80%80%E3%83%BC%E5%BE%A1%E7%A5%9E%E9%85%92%E9%A0%82%E6%88%B4%E5%BC%8F%E3%83%BC/ | title= 祇園祭を振り返って　ー御神酒頂戴式ー | website= ましこサポーターズクラブ | date=2013-8-1 | accessdate=2023-7-26}}「下野新聞」2006年（平成18年）7月25日付 26面「6.5ﾘｯﾄﾙの大杯 飲み干す」「酒豪集い「御神酒頂戴式」」「益子町」{{Cite instagram|user=onaidashigumi |author=城内山車組 |postid=CvEUMGRy-xa |title=令和5年 益子祇園祭 2日目　御神酒頂戴式……… |date=2023-07-24|access-date=2024-07-26}}。 最終日の[[7月25日]]は、御仮屋から八坂神社へ神輿による「還御祭」が斎行される。午後には「花馬」を先頭に、各町の山車による合同運行が催され[https://www.facebook.com/story.php?id=100068795282898&story_fbid=1365104486920942 【花馬合流】石並から花馬合流です………｜益子観光協会 - Facebook] 2017年7月25日投稿。2024年7月30日閲覧。{{Twitter status|mashiko_kankou|1022000470217379841|【益子祇園祭　屋台合同運行・花馬合流】 益子町の石並公民館から、花馬が益子駅南側にて屋台と合流し鹿島神社まで運行されました。……… - Twitter}} 2018年7月25日投稿。2024年7月30日閲覧。{{Cite instagram|user=kashima1115 |author=益子 鹿島神社【公式】 |postid=CvG9vugO5ZK |title=花馬 合同運行……… |date=2023-07-25|access-date=2024-07-28}}{{Cite instagram|user=kashima1115 |author=益子 鹿島神社【公式】 |postid=CvG-ADWNEC4 |title=花馬 合同運行……… |date=2023-07-25|access-date=2024-07-28}}、福笹が授与される{{Cite instagram|user=kashima1115 |author=益子 鹿島神社【公式】 |postid=CvG-VVWsKN1 |title=福笹の授与……… |date=2023-07-25|access-date=2024-07-28}}。そして夜22時からは、益子町の鹿島神社の前に各町の山車が全て集い、「御上覧」と呼ばれる山車の神前奉納の儀が行われる{{sfn|柏村祐司,栃木の祭り|2012|p=106-108}}{{Cite instagram|user=kashima1115 |author=益子 鹿島神社【公式】 |postid=CvICNJlgIGc |title=塙 御上覧……… |date=2023-07-26|access-date=2024-07-30}}{{Cite instagram|user=kashima1115 |author=益子 鹿島神社【公式】 |postid=CvIDZBytRP0 |title=御上覧 〆……… |date=2023-07-26|access-date=2024-07-30}}。 == 新型コロナ禍の影響 == [[2019年]]末（令和元年末）に発生した[[新型コロナ禍]]により、[[2020年]]（令和2年）は益子祇園祭の一連の行事は中止された。益子町の各町代表者が八坂神社末社がある益子鹿島神社に集まり、神事が執り行われた{{Cite instagram|user=onaidashigumi |author=城内山車組 |postid=CC-GeOCnFBe |title=本来であれば、今日7月23日は益子祇園祭の初日でした。……… |date=2020-07-23|access-date=2024-07-26}}。 翌年以降も[[山車]]練り歩きはされたものの、「御上覧」における鹿島神社前での[[屋台]]の出店取り止めや、感染対策が取られた上での御神酒頂戴式などの規模縮小の措置が取られた[https://mokashinbun.com/area_news/%E7%9B%8A%E5%AD%90%E7%94%BA%E7%A5%87%E5%9C%92%E7%A5%AD%E8%A6%8F%E6%A8%A1%E7%B8%AE%E5%B0%8F%E3%81%A7%E9%96%8B%E5%82%AC/ 益子町祇園祭規模縮小で開催 | 真岡新聞]{{リンク切れ|date=2024年7月}}。そして[[2023年]]（令和5年）[[7月23日]]から[[7月25日]]まで、屋台も出店したほぼ完全な形での益子祇園祭が4年ぶりに行われた{{Efn|現在も「[[御神酒頂戴式]]」では新型コロナ感染対策により神事の簡略化が行われている}}。 == エピソード == * [[2005年]]（平成17年）、｢あじさい祭り｣を取りまとめる｢あじさい会｣の会長となった[[益子町]]の陶器ギャラリー｢佳乃や｣店主・小峰彰により、陶芸家であり画家であり版画家でもあった[[糸井哲夫]]に｢益子祇園祭のうちわ｣の版画絵製作が依頼され{{sfn|ミチカケ 第5号,益子町|2015|p=8,10,12}}、同年の｢あじさい会｣から始まり{{sfn|ミチカケ 第5号,益子町|2015|p=10}}、[[2006年]]（平成18年）には城内{{sfn|ミチカケ 第5号,益子町|2015|p=7}}、[[2007年]]（平成19年）に内町{{sfn|ミチカケ 第5号,益子町|2015|p=9}}、[[2008年]]（平成20年）に新町{{sfn|ミチカケ 第5号,益子町|2015|p=11}}、[[2009年]]（平成21年）に田町{{sfn|ミチカケ 第5号,益子町|2015|p=13}}、そして[[2010年]]（平成22年）の道祖土まで{{sfn|ミチカケ 第5号,益子町|2015|p=15}}の各当番町のうちわ版画絵が製作された{{sfn|ミチカケ 第5号,益子町|2015|p=12}}{{sfn|ミチカケ 第5号,益子町|2015|p=6-15}}。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} === 注釈 === {{notelist}} === 出典 === {{Reflist|2}} == 参考文献 == * {{cite book|和書|author=柏村祐司|editor=|title=栃木の祭り|publisher=有限会社 随想社|series=|date=2012-11-15|pages=13,106-108|NCID=|id=|isbn=9784887482708|ref={{sfnref|柏村祐司,栃木の祭り|2012}}}} * {{cite book|和書|author=|title=ミチカケ 益子の人と暮らしを伝える 第5号（2015 秋）|publisher=益子町|date=2015-09-20|pages=|NCID=|id=[https://kensaku.lib.pref.tochigi.lg.jp/winj/opac/switch-detail-iccap.do?bibid=1102070474 栃木県立図書館検索結果],[https://kensaku.lib.pref.tochigi.lg.jp/winj/opac/switch-detail-iccap.do?bibid=2200179102 栃木県立図書館検索結果]|ref={{sfnref|ミチカケ 第5号,益子町|2015}}}} :：[https://mashiko.town/library/michikake05/ 『ミチカケ』第5号インターネットアーカイヴ] :：冒頭からP30にかけて、｢益子祇園祭｣を中心に｢益子町の祭り｣について特集されている。 === 「益子祇園祭」に関わる人物の記事が掲載されている文献 === * {{cite book|和書|author=倉本秀清|title=益子探訪　－益子の陶芸家に学ぶ－|publisher=株式会社 光芸出版|date=1992-07-05|series=|page=72-74|isbn=4769400977|ref={{sfnref|益子探訪,倉本秀清|1992}}}} * {{cite book|和書|author=下野新聞社|title=陶源境ましこ 益子の陶工 人と作品|date=1984-09-27|pages=94-95,135|NCID=BN1293471X|id={{NDLSearch|R100000001-I25110924685}}|ref={{sfnref|下野新聞社|1984}}}} * {{cite book|和書|author=|title=焼物の里を訪ねて　益子・笠間　器の里、最新ガイド。|publisher=株式会社 枻出版社|date=2009-10-20|series=エイムック 1816|pages=86-89|isbn=9784777914579|ref={{sfnref|枻出版社,焼き物の里を訪ねて益子・笠間|2009}}}} == 外部リンク == === 益子祇園祭 === * [http://www.mashiko-kankou.org/culture/traditional_culture.shtml 益子の文化｜伝統文化｜祇園祭]-[http://www.mashiko-kankou.org/ 益子町観光協会] * [https://www.town.mashiko.lg.jp/sp/page/page002973.html 「ましこ世間遺産」認定No.54｜八坂神社祇園祭（益子祇園祭・天王祭）] - [https://www.town.mashiko.lg.jp/sp/ 益子町公式ホームページ] * [https://tabi-mag.jp/mashiko-gion/ 益子祇園祭｜益子町] - [https://tabi-mag.jp/ ニッポン旅マガジン] * [https://matsuri-no-hi.com/amp_matsuri/56882 益子祇園祭 -2023年-] - [https://matsuri-no-hi.com/ 祭の日] === 益子鹿島神社 === * {{Instagram|kashima1115|益子 鹿島神社【公式】}} === 御神酒頂戴式 === * [https://www.town.mashiko.lg.jp/cultural_property.php?mode=detail&code=20 御神酒頂戴式｜文化財詳細｜益子町の文化財] - [https://www.town.mashiko.lg.jp/sp/ 益子町公式ホームページ] === 下野手筒会 === * {{Instagram|shimotsuketedutsu|下野手筒会}} * {{Instagram|kinta_studio0105|永田茂夫 作家名 KINTA}} === 益子町山車組 === * {{Instagram|jonaidashigumi|城内坂山車組}} {{Event-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:ましこきおんさい}} [[Category:栃木県の祭り]] [[Category:祇園祭・天王祭]] [[Category:益子町]] revision.comment: Copying from [[Category:Ja import oversized]] to [[Category:jawiki oversized to be reviewed]] using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
'''Mashiko Gion Festival''' (益子祇園祭) is a festival held annually from {{ill|July 23|ja|7月23日}} to {{ill|July 25|ja|7月25日}} in {{ill|Mashiko, Tochigi|ja|益子町}}, Tochigi Prefecture. It is the festival of {{ill|Yasaka Shrine (Mashiko)|ja|八坂神社}}, which is a subordinate shrine within the grounds of {{ill|Kashima Shrine (Mashiko)|ja|鹿島神社 (益子町)}}<ref>[https://www.town.mashiko.lg.jp/sp/page/page001983.html "Mashiko Sekai Heritage" No.10｜Yasaka Shrine - Mashiko Town Official Website]</ref><ref name="MashikoJouhouKyoku2012_07_24">{{Cite web | url= http://mashikojk.blog.fc2.com/blog-entry-19.html | title= Mashiko's Summer - Gion Festival Departure Ceremony & Handheld Fireworks Dedication | website= Mashiko Information Bureau | date= 2012-7-24 | accessdate=2024-8-1}}</ref><ref name="TouKo_MashikoGionMaturi_2023">{{Cite instagram|user=tokomashiko |author=Tōko / Mashiko Pottery Gallery |postid=CvOdm-FPlD_ |title=Reiwa 5th Year Mashiko Gion Festival was held from July 23 to 25... |date=2023-07-28|access-date=2024-07-26}}</ref><ref name="Mashiko_no_Omatsuri">[http://www.mashiko-kankou.org/culture/traditional_culture.shtml Mashiko Culture - Traditional Culture | Mashiko Tourism Association]</ref><ref name="MashikoGionMatsuri_MashikoSekenIsan_054">{{Cite web | url= https://www.town.mashiko.lg.jp/sp/page/page002973.html | title= Mashiko Sekai Heritage No.54｜Yasaka Shrine Gion Festival (Mashiko Gion Festival / Tenno Festival) | website= Mashiko Town Official Website | date= | accessdate=2024-7-30}}</ref>.
One of the most famous events is the '''"Omiki Choudai-shiki"''' (御神酒頂戴式), a ritual for passing the town's duty, which is considered one of the '''"Three Great Peculiar Festivals of Kanto"''' and is held on the middle day, {{ill|July 24|ja|7月24日}}<ref name=MashikoGionMatsuri_MashikoSekenIsan_054/><ref name=TouKo_MashikoGionMaturi_2023/>{{sfn|Kashiwamura Yūji, Festivals of Tochigi|2012|p=106-108}}.
== Overview == The '''Yasaka Shrine''' in Mashiko is a subordinate shrine of {{ill|Kashima Shrine (Mashiko)|ja|鹿島神社 (益子町)}}<ref name=MashikoGionMatsuri_MashikoSekenIsan_054/>. Every year from '''July 23 to July 25''', the Gion Festival ('''formerly called Tenno Festival''') is held<ref name=MashikoJouhouKyoku2012_07_24/><ref name=MashikoGionMatsuri_MashikoSekenIsan_054/>.
The festival is also known as '''"Yasaka Shrine Gion Festival"'''{{sfn|Kashiwamura Yūji, Festivals of Tochigi|2012|p=106-108}}.
The central town districts of Mashiko, such as '''Shinmachi, Tamachi, Uchimachi, Jōnai, and Dōsodo''', take turns being the hosting town. These five towns, along with '''Hanawa District''', participate with '''festival floats''' that parade through Mashiko’s main streets in a grand celebration{{sfn|Mashiko Exploration, Kuramoto Hidekiyo|1992|p=72}}<ref name=MashikoJouhouKyoku2012_07_24/>.
Many participants are not just original Mashiko residents but also '''potters who have moved to Mashiko''', including '''Takauchi Hideyuki''' and '''Susukida Kōji'''{{sfn|Mashiko Exploration, Kuramoto Hidekiyo|1992|p=72-74}}{{sfn|Shimotsuke Newspaper|1984|p=94-95}}<ref name=SusukidaKouji_no_DeshiTachi>{{Cite instagram|user=ito137.3life |author=ito_susukida |postid=BbiaHMtg5kk |title=Father and his seven apprentices... |date=2017-11-18 |access-date=2023-07-19}}</ref>.
== Origin == Around '''Hōei 2 (1705)''', an '''epidemic''' broke out in Mashiko. To pray for its eradication, the '''Gion belief''' (祇園信仰) originating from Kyoto in the Heian period was introduced{{sfn|Michikake Vol.5, Mashiko Town|2015|p=14}}, and people began '''worshiping Gozu Tennō''', the deity of plagues<ref name=MashikoJouhouKyoku2012_07_24/><ref name=TouKo_MashikoGionMaturi_2023/>{{sfn|Kashiwamura Yūji, Festivals of Tochigi|2012|p=106-108}}.
---
=== '''Category & External Links'''=== * [http://www.mashiko-kankou.org/culture/traditional_culture.shtml Mashiko Culture | Traditional Culture | Gion Festival] * '''Mashiko Tourism Association''' * [https://www.town.mashiko.lg.jp/sp/page/page002973.html Mashiko Sekai Heritage No.54 | Yasaka Shrine Gion Festival] * '''Mashiko Town Official Website''' * [https://tabi-mag.jp/mashiko-gion/ Mashiko Gion Festival] * '''Nippon Tabi Magazine'''

revision.text:
'''Mashiko Gion Festival''' (益子祇園祭) is a festival held annually from {{ill|July 23|ja|7月23日}} to {{ill|July 25|ja|7月25日}} in {{ill|Mashiko, Tochigi|ja|益子町}}, Tochigi Prefecture. It is the festival of {{ill|Yasaka Shrine (Mashiko)|ja|八坂神社}}, which is a subordinate shrine within the grounds of {{ill|Kashima Shrine (Mashiko)|ja|鹿島神社 (益子町)}}<ref>[https://www.town.mashiko.lg.jp/sp/page/page001983.html "Mashiko Sekai Heritage" No.10｜Yasaka Shrine - Mashiko Town Official Website]</ref><ref name="MashikoJouhouKyoku2012_07_24">{{Cite web | url= http://mashikojk.blog.fc2.com/blog-entry-19.html | title= Mashiko's Summer - Gion Festival Departure Ceremony & Handheld Fireworks Dedication | website= Mashiko Information Bureau | date= 2012-7-24 | accessdate=2024-8-1}}</ref><ref name="TouKo_MashikoGionMaturi_2023">{{Cite instagram|user=tokomashiko |author=Tōko / Mashiko Pottery Gallery |postid=CvOdm-FPlD_ |title=Reiwa 5th Year Mashiko Gion Festival was held from July 23 to 25... |date=2023-07-28|access-date=2024-07-26}}</ref><ref name="Mashiko_no_Omatsuri">[http://www.mashiko-kankou.org/culture/traditional_culture.shtml Mashiko Culture - Traditional Culture | Mashiko Tourism Association]</ref><ref name="MashikoGionMatsuri_MashikoSekenIsan_054">{{Cite web | url= https://www.town.mashiko.lg.jp/sp/page/page002973.html | title= Mashiko Sekai Heritage No.54｜Yasaka Shrine Gion Festival (Mashiko Gion Festival / Tenno Festival) | website= Mashiko Town Official Website | date= | accessdate=2024-7-30}}</ref>.
One of the most famous events is the '''"Omiki Choudai-shiki"''' (御神酒頂戴式), a ritual for passing the town's duty, which is considered one of the '''"Three Great Peculiar Festivals of Kanto"''' and is held on the middle day, {{ill|July 24|ja|7月24日}}<ref name=MashikoGionMatsuri_MashikoSekenIsan_054/><ref name=TouKo_MashikoGionMaturi_2023/>{{sfn|Kashiwamura Yūji, Festivals of Tochigi|2012|p=106-108}}.
== Overview == The '''Yasaka Shrine''' in Mashiko is a subordinate shrine of {{ill|Kashima Shrine (Mashiko)|ja|鹿島神社 (益子町)}}<ref name=MashikoGionMatsuri_MashikoSekenIsan_054/>. Every year from '''July 23 to July 25''', the Gion Festival ('''formerly called Tenno Festival''') is held<ref name=MashikoJouhouKyoku2012_07_24/><ref name=MashikoGionMatsuri_MashikoSekenIsan_054/>.
The festival is also known as '''"Yasaka Shrine Gion Festival"'''{{sfn|Kashiwamura Yūji, Festivals of Tochigi|2012|p=106-108}}.
The central town districts of Mashiko, such as '''Shinmachi, Tamachi, Uchimachi, Jōnai, and Dōsodo''', take turns being the hosting town. These five towns, along with '''Hanawa District''', participate with '''festival floats''' that parade through Mashiko’s main streets in a grand celebration{{sfn|Mashiko Exploration, Kuramoto Hidekiyo|1992|p=72}}<ref name=MashikoJouhouKyoku2012_07_24/>.
Many participants are not just original Mashiko residents but also '''potters who have moved to Mashiko''', including '''Takauchi Hideyuki''' and '''Susukida Kōji'''{{sfn|Mashiko Exploration, Kuramoto Hidekiyo|1992|p=72-74}}{{sfn|Shimotsuke Newspaper|1984|p=94-95}}<ref name=SusukidaKouji_no_DeshiTachi>{{Cite instagram|user=ito137.3life |author=ito_susukida |postid=BbiaHMtg5kk |title=Father and his seven apprentices... |date=2017-11-18 |access-date=2023-07-19}}</ref>.
== Origin == Around '''Hōei 2 (1705)''', an '''epidemic''' broke out in Mashiko. To pray for its eradication, the '''Gion belief''' (祇園信仰) originating from Kyoto in the Heian period was introduced{{sfn|Michikake Vol.5, Mashiko Town|2015|p=14}}, and people began '''worshiping Gozu Tennō''', the deity of plagues<ref name=MashikoJouhouKyoku2012_07_24/><ref name=TouKo_MashikoGionMaturi_2023/>{{sfn|Kashiwamura Yūji, Festivals of Tochigi|2012|p=106-108}}.
---
=== '''Category & External Links'''=== * [http://www.mashiko-kankou.org/culture/traditional_culture.shtml Mashiko Culture | Traditional Culture | Gion Festival] * '''Mashiko Tourism Association''' * [https://www.town.mashiko.lg.jp/sp/page/page002973.html Mashiko Sekai Heritage No.54 | Yasaka Shrine Gion Festival] * '''Mashiko Town Official Website''' * [https://tabi-mag.jp/mashiko-gion/ Mashiko Gion Festival] * '''Nippon Tabi Magazine'''

revision.text:
{{Pathnav|栃木県|益子町|益子祇園祭|hide = 1|frame=1}} {{イベントインフォメーション |イベント名称 = 益子祇園祭 |英文表記 = |画像 = |画像サイズ = |画像説明 = |種類 = 祇園祭（山王祭） |通称 =（益子訛りの方言として）ギョン祭り{{sfn|益子探訪,倉本秀清|1992|p=72}} |正式名称 = |旧名称 = |開催時期 = [[7月23日]]、[[7月24日|24日]]、[[7月25日|25日]] |初回開催 = |最終開催 = |開催時間 |会場 = [[栃木県]][[益子町]]町内の各所。
城内町、田町、道祖土町などの各参加町の主要道路。
栃木県益子町鹿島神社前。 |主催 = |共催 = |後援 = |協賛 = |企画制作 = |協力 = |運営 = |プロデューサー = |出展数 = |来場者数 = |会場アクセス名 = |最寄駅 =[[益子駅]] |直通バス = |駐車場 = |URL = |特記事項 = }} '''益子祇園祭'''（ましこぎおんまつり）は、[[栃木県]][[芳賀郡]][[益子町]]で毎年[[7月23日]]から[[7月25日]]の3日間、益子にある[[鹿島神社 (益子町)|鹿島神社]]の境内末社である八坂神社[https://www.town.mashiko.lg.jp/sp/page/page001983.html 「ましこ世間遺産」認定No.10｜八坂神社-益子町公式ホームページ]の祭礼として行われる祭りである{{Cite web | url= http://mashikojk.blog.fc2.com/blog-entry-19.html | title= 益子の夏～祇園祭　-出御祭・手筒花火奉納- | website= 益子情報局 | date= 2012-7-24 | accessdate=2024-8-1}}{{Cite instagram|user=tokomashiko |author=陶庫／益子焼 陶器ギャラリー |postid=CvOdm-FPlD_ |title=令和五年度益子祇園祭が7月23日～25日に斎行されました。……… |date=2023-07-28|access-date=2024-07-26}}[http://www.mashiko-kankou.org/culture/traditional_culture.shtml 益子の文化 伝統文化｜益子町観光協会]{{Cite web | url= https://www.town.mashiko.lg.jp/sp/page/page002973.html | title= ましこ世間遺産 認定No.54｜八坂神社祇園祭（益子祇園祭・天王祭）　町政｜町の紹介 | website= 益子町 公式ホームページ | date= | accessdate=2024-7-30}}。 中日の[[7月24日]]に、「当番町引き継ぎの儀式」である神事の一つとして「関東三大奇祭」の一つとされることもある「'''[[御神酒頂戴式]]'''」が行われることで有名である{{sfn|柏村祐司,栃木の祭り|2012|p=106-108}}。 == 概要 == 益子町にある八坂神社は、同じく益子町にある鹿島神社の境内末社である。そして毎年、7月23日、24日、25日の3日間、祇園祭（過去には天王祭と呼ばれていた）が行われる。 益子の祇園祭は「八坂神社の祇園祭」とも呼ばれている{{sfn|柏村祐司,栃木の祭り|2012|p=106-108}}。 益子の中心部にある氏子地区である新町、田町、内町、城内、道祖土が当番制で当番町を務め、この各5町と塙地区からそれぞれ[[山車]]が参加し、益子町の中心街を練り歩く盛大な祭りである{{sfn|益子探訪,倉本秀清|1992|p=72}}。 もともとの益子町民のみならず、[[高内秀剛]]や{{sfn|益子探訪,倉本秀清|1992|p=72-74}}[[薄田浩司]]など、益子町に移住した[[陶芸家]]の中にも積極的に参加する者もいる{{sfn|下野新聞社|1984|p=94-95}}{{Cite instagram|user=ito137.3life |author=ito_susukida 薄田いと |postid=BbiaHMtg5kk |title=父と父のもとで修行した7名とで… |date=2017-11-18 |access-date=2023-07-19}}。 == 由来 == [[宝永]]2年（[[1705年]]）頃、益子に疫病が流行った際に、平安時代の京都から伝わる天王信仰（[[祇園信仰]]）に基づき{{sfn|ミチカケ 第5号,益子町|2015|p=14}}、疫病退散を祈り疫病を司る[[牛頭天王]]を祭ったことから始まったとされている{{sfn|柏村祐司,栃木の祭り|2012|p=106-108}}。 == 昔の祭事日程と内容 == 昔は[[陰暦]]に基づいて祭事が行われていた[{{NDLDC|815437/17}} 『下野神社沿革誌 巻之6』風山広雄 編「益子町」「益子町大字益子鎮座」「村社 鹿島神社」十五 - 十六 - 国立国会図書館デジタルコレクション]　2023年9月23日閲覧。 陰暦の6月15日に仮屋への神輿の橋渡しが行われる。 そして陰暦6月23日から大祭が挙行される。 陰暦6月23日には[[氏子]]一同に神酒が賜る。当番引き継ぎには、当時の一年の日数に合わせた三升六合入りの大杯を用いて神酒を賜った。これが現在の「[[御神酒頂戴式]]」へと繋がっている。 陰暦6月24日には神輿の入御が行われ、昔は警衛士、大旗、騎馬乗馬の鎧武者、神主、靴持ち、道具持ち、村役人などによる行列式が行われた。 そして陰暦6月25日には、当時の益子村の領主であった[[黒羽藩]]主・[[大関氏]]へ、神主からお祓い献上の儀式が行われた。 == 日程と各種行事 == === あじさい祭り === 6月末に、益子町の｢あじさい公園｣内にある｢太平神社｣で{{Cite web | url= http://blog.mashiko-kankou.org/staff/?p=22545 | title= あじさい祭り（2024年6月24日～7月25日まで）｜お知らせ | website= 益子観光協会 | date= 2024-6-1 | accessdate=2024-8-1}}、益子祇園祭シーズンの始まりを告げる｢あじさい祭り｣が開かれる{{sfn|ミチカケ 第5号,益子町|2015|p=8}}。 [[稚児#祭りにおける稚児|稚児]]が[[巫女]]に扮し[[紫陽花]]を献花する｢献花祭｣と 、境内に作られた｢茅の輪｣をくぐり厄を祓う｢[[大祓#茅の輪くぐり|茅の輪くぐり]]・[[大祓|大祓式]]｣が執り行われる。 また境内と参道に飾られる[[提灯]]は、｢益子祇園祭｣が終わる7月25日まで灯し続けられる。 === 手筒花火：下野手筒会 === [[2005年]]（平成17年）から採用され「下野新聞」2005年（平成17年）7月22日付 11面「手筒花火を披露」「24日に益子祇園祭」「本場・愛知で習ってきました」「自営業者の会が実演」「下野新聞」2006年（平成18年）7月22日付 31面「手筒花火を今年も披露」「祇園祭の24日」「下野新聞」2006年（平成18年）7月26日付 26面「火の粉浴びながら」「益子の下野手筒会」「勇壮な打ち上げ披露」、益子町在住の造形家・KINTA[http://www14.plala.or.jp/KINTA-STUDIO/info/info.html KINTA STUDIO【ご案内】][http://www14.plala.or.jp/KINTA-STUDIO/about/profile.html KINTA／キンタ【プロフィール】]{{Instagram|kinta_studio0105|永田茂夫 作家名 KINTA}}{{sfn|枻出版社,焼き物の里を訪ねて益子・笠間|2009|p=86-89}}を中心として結成された「下野手筒会」により[http://www14.plala.or.jp/KINTA-STUDIO/about/mashiko/tedutsu-kai02.html 下野手筒会について][http://www14.plala.or.jp/KINTA-STUDIO/about/mashiko/tedutsu-kai.html 下野手筒会【しもつけてづつかい】]、益子祇園祭の初日となる[[7月23日]]の夜{{Efn|出典資料の通り、当初は益子祇園祭の2日目となる[[7月24日]]に披露されていた。}}に｢[[手筒花火]]｣が打ち上げられていた{{sfn|柏村祐司,栃木の祭り|2012|p=106-108}}{{Cite web|和書 | url= https://www.shimotsuke.co.jp/articles/-/767424 | title= 雨のように降り注ぐ火の粉に歓声　益子祇園祭で復活の手筒花火 | website= 下野新聞「SOON」 | date=2023-7-24 | accessdate=2023-7-26}}。 その後、[[2024年]]（令和6年）より、益子祇園祭の一環として、祭開催直前の土曜日に披露されることになった{{Cite web | url= http://blog.mashiko-kankou.org/staff/?p=22605 | title= 手筒花火（2024年7月20日開催）｜お知らせ | website= 益子観光協会 | date= 2024-6-14 | accessdate=2024-8-1}}{{Cite web | url= https://www.shimotsuke.co.jp/articles/-/927973 | title= 空に迫力満点の火柱を　地元有志が２０日夜に手筒花火を披露へ　益子 | website= 下野新聞 SOON | date= 2024-7-18 | accessdate=2024-8-1}}{{Cite web | url= https://kitakan-navi.jp/archives/209416 | title= 栃木県益子町で7月20日に手筒花火 迫力満点！高さ8mの火柱が上がる“炎の祭典”を間近で | website= きたかんナビ | date= 2024-7-19 | accessdate=2024-8-1}}{{Cite web | url= https://www.shimotsuke.co.jp/articles/-/929373 | title= 夜空を赤く、熱く照らす炎　益子で「手筒花火」　８メートルの火柱、観客魅了 | website= 下野新聞 SOON | date= 2024-7-20 | accessdate=2024-8-1}}。 === 益子祇園祭 === 日付は曜日とは関係無く、日程を変更すること無く、毎年7月23日、24日、25日の日程で開催される。 1日目となる[[7月23日]]、八坂神社から御分霊された御霊を八坂神社の本社神輿に乗せて担ぎ {{Cite instagram|user=onaidashigumi |author=城内山車組 |postid=CvBsiHeJj9d |title=令和5年 益子祇園祭 初日　始まりました！……… |date=2023-07-23|access-date=2024-07-26}}、[[天狗]]の面を被った[[猿田彦]]により先導され、その年の当番となった地区に設置された「御仮屋」に配置する「神輿の渡御：出御祭」から始まる{{Cite instagram|user=tokomashiko |author=陶庫／益子焼 陶器ギャラリー |postid=CvBtqMwPzH7 |title=御仮屋が陶庫にでき、神様がいらっしゃいました。……… |date=2023-07-23|access-date=2024-07-26}}{{sfn|柏村祐司,栃木の祭り|2012|p=106-108}}{{Cite instagram|user=kashima1115 |author=益子 鹿島神社【公式】 |postid=CvG9cOGsTsg |title=八坂神社 神輿御渡……… |date=2023-07-25|access-date=2024-07-28}}。 そして益子町内の氏子地区である5地区：新町{{Cite instagram|user=kashima1115 |author=益子 鹿島神社【公式】 |postid=CvIBKZvgI7L |title=新町 御上覧……… |date=2023-07-26|access-date=2024-07-30}}・田町{{Cite instagram|user=kashima1115 |author=益子 鹿島神社【公式】 |postid=CvIAwRcsZgJ |title=田町 御上覧……… |date=2023-07-26|access-date=2024-07-30}}・内町{{Cite instagram|user=kashima1115 |author=益子 鹿島神社【公式】 |postid=CvIAG10O5UQ |title=内町 御上覧……… |date=2023-07-26|access-date=2024-07-30}}・城内{{Cite instagram|user=kashima1115 |author=益子 鹿島神社【公式】 |postid=CvH_FTdLERS |title=城内 御上覧……… |date=2023-07-26|access-date=2024-07-30}}・道祖土{{Cite instagram|user=kashima1115 |author=益子 鹿島神社【公式】 |postid=CvIB9fOMp3k |title=道祖土 御上覧……… |date=2023-07-26|access-date=2024-07-30}}と、田野地区の山本を入れる6地区の[[山車]]が「付け祭り」として{{sfn|柏村祐司,栃木の祭り|2012|p=106-108}}益子町内を巡行し、益子の町中を練り歩く{{Cite instagram|user=onaidashigumi |author=城内山車組 |postid=CvCmWtcywKF |title=令和5年 益子祇園祭 初日　無事運行終了しました……… |date=2023-07-23|access-date=2024-07-26}}{{Cite instagram|user=onaidashigumi |author=城内山車組 |postid=CvFYmiKSV6x |title=令和5年 益子祇園祭 2日目　本日も沢山の方々のご協力で、安全に運行することが出来ました。……… |date=2023-07-24|access-date=2024-07-26}}{{Cite instagram|user=onaidashigumi |author=城内山車組 |postid=CvIQ7ioSJ6f |title=令和5年 益子祇園祭 最終日……… |date=2023-07-26|access-date=2024-07-26}}。これは期間中3日間、他の各種行事と平行しながら続けられる。 ちなみに「御神酒頂戴式」は女人禁制であるが、益子祇園祭の牛頭天王は「女性」と言われているためか{{sfn|柏村祐司,栃木の祭り|2012|p=106-108}}、山車の巡行やお囃子の演奏にも女子や女性が積極的に参加する。そして他の地域の祇園祭のような、山車同士でぶつかり合っての荒々しい揉み合いは行われない{{sfn|柏村祐司,栃木の祭り|2012|p=106-108}}。 2日目となる[[7月24日]]には、[[江戸時代]]から伝わる益子町指定[[無形民俗文化財]]であり、[[栃木県]][[日光市]][[輪王寺]]の「[[輪王寺#年中行事|強飯式]]」を代表とする、栃木県に多く存在する「強飯習俗」のうちでも「本物の[[酒]]」を飲むことを強いる、全国でも稀な「当番引き継ぎ式」となる{{sfn|柏村祐司,栃木の祭り|2012|p=106-108}}ことから関東三大奇祭の一つとされている「[[御神酒頂戴式|八坂神社御神酒頂戴式]]（やさかじんじゃおみきちょうだいしき）」が行われる{{sfn|柏村祐司,栃木の祭り|2012|p=106-108}}[https://www.town.mashiko.lg.jp/cultural_property.php?mode=detail&code=20 八坂神社御神酒頂戴式御神酒頂戴式｜文化財詳細｜益子町の文化財 - 益子町公式ホームページ]{{Cite web|和書 | url= https://mashikosc.wordpress.com/2013/08/01/%E7%A5%87%E5%9C%92%E7%A5%AD%E3%82%92%E6%8C%AF%E3%82%8A%E8%BF%94%E3%81%A3%E3%81%A6%E3%80%80%E3%83%BC%E5%BE%A1%E7%A5%9E%E9%85%92%E9%A0%82%E6%88%B4%E5%BC%8F%E3%83%BC/ | title= 祇園祭を振り返って　ー御神酒頂戴式ー | website= ましこサポーターズクラブ | date=2013-8-1 | accessdate=2023-7-26}}「下野新聞」2006年（平成18年）7月25日付 26面「6.5ﾘｯﾄﾙの大杯 飲み干す」「酒豪集い「御神酒頂戴式」」「益子町」{{Cite instagram|user=onaidashigumi |author=城内山車組 |postid=CvEUMGRy-xa |title=令和5年 益子祇園祭 2日目　御神酒頂戴式……… |date=2023-07-24|access-date=2024-07-26}}。 最終日の[[7月25日]]は、御仮屋から八坂神社へ神輿による「還御祭」が斎行される。午後には「花馬」を先頭に、各町の山車による合同運行が催され[https://www.facebook.com/story.php?id=100068795282898&story_fbid=1365104486920942 【花馬合流】石並から花馬合流です………｜益子観光協会 - Facebook] 2017年7月25日投稿。2024年7月30日閲覧。{{Twitter status|mashiko_kankou|1022000470217379841|【益子祇園祭　屋台合同運行・花馬合流】 益子町の石並公民館から、花馬が益子駅南側にて屋台と合流し鹿島神社まで運行されました。……… - Twitter}} 2018年7月25日投稿。2024年7月30日閲覧。{{Cite instagram|user=kashima1115 |author=益子 鹿島神社【公式】 |postid=CvG9vugO5ZK |title=花馬 合同運行……… |date=2023-07-25|access-date=2024-07-28}}{{Cite instagram|user=kashima1115 |author=益子 鹿島神社【公式】 |postid=CvG-ADWNEC4 |title=花馬 合同運行……… |date=2023-07-25|access-date=2024-07-28}}、福笹が授与される{{Cite instagram|user=kashima1115 |author=益子 鹿島神社【公式】 |postid=CvG-VVWsKN1 |title=福笹の授与……… |date=2023-07-25|access-date=2024-07-28}}。そして夜22時からは、益子町の鹿島神社の前に各町の山車が全て集い、「御上覧」と呼ばれる山車の神前奉納の儀が行われる{{sfn|柏村祐司,栃木の祭り|2012|p=106-108}}{{Cite instagram|user=kashima1115 |author=益子 鹿島神社【公式】 |postid=CvICNJlgIGc |title=塙 御上覧……… |date=2023-07-26|access-date=2024-07-30}}{{Cite instagram|user=kashima1115 |author=益子 鹿島神社【公式】 |postid=CvIDZBytRP0 |title=御上覧 〆……… |date=2023-07-26|access-date=2024-07-30}}。 == 新型コロナ禍の影響 == [[2019年]]末（令和元年末）に発生した[[新型コロナ禍]]により、[[2020年]]（令和2年）は益子祇園祭の一連の行事は中止された。益子町の各町代表者が八坂神社末社がある益子鹿島神社に集まり、神事が執り行われた{{Cite instagram|user=onaidashigumi |author=城内山車組 |postid=CC-GeOCnFBe |title=本来であれば、今日7月23日は益子祇園祭の初日でした。……… |date=2020-07-23|access-date=2024-07-26}}。 翌年以降も[[山車]]練り歩きはされたものの、「御上覧」における鹿島神社前での[[屋台]]の出店取り止めや、感染対策が取られた上での御神酒頂戴式などの規模縮小の措置が取られた[https://mokashinbun.com/area_news/%E7%9B%8A%E5%AD%90%E7%94%BA%E7%A5%87%E5%9C%92%E7%A5%AD%E8%A6%8F%E6%A8%A1%E7%B8%AE%E5%B0%8F%E3%81%A7%E9%96%8B%E5%82%AC/ 益子町祇園祭規模縮小で開催 | 真岡新聞]{{リンク切れ|date=2024年7月}}。そして[[2023年]]（令和5年）[[7月23日]]から[[7月25日]]まで、屋台も出店したほぼ完全な形での益子祇園祭が4年ぶりに行われた{{Efn|現在も「[[御神酒頂戴式]]」では新型コロナ感染対策により神事の簡略化が行われている}}。 == エピソード == * [[2005年]]（平成17年）、｢あじさい祭り｣を取りまとめる｢あじさい会｣の会長となった[[益子町]]の陶器ギャラリー｢佳乃や｣店主・小峰彰により、陶芸家であり画家であり版画家でもあった[[糸井哲夫]]に｢益子祇園祭のうちわ｣の版画絵製作が依頼され{{sfn|ミチカケ 第5号,益子町|2015|p=8,10,12}}、同年の｢あじさい会｣から始まり{{sfn|ミチカケ 第5号,益子町|2015|p=10}}、[[2006年]]（平成18年）には城内{{sfn|ミチカケ 第5号,益子町|2015|p=7}}、[[2007年]]（平成19年）に内町{{sfn|ミチカケ 第5号,益子町|2015|p=9}}、[[2008年]]（平成20年）に新町{{sfn|ミチカケ 第5号,益子町|2015|p=11}}、[[2009年]]（平成21年）に田町{{sfn|ミチカケ 第5号,益子町|2015|p=13}}、そして[[2010年]]（平成22年）の道祖土まで{{sfn|ミチカケ 第5号,益子町|2015|p=15}}の各当番町のうちわ版画絵が製作された{{sfn|ミチカケ 第5号,益子町|2015|p=12}}{{sfn|ミチカケ 第5号,益子町|2015|p=6-15}}。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} === 注釈 === {{notelist}} '''Mashiko Gion Festival''' (益子祇園祭) is a festival held annually from {{ill|July 23|ja|7月23日}} to {{ill|July 25|ja|7月25日}} in {{ill|Mashiko, Tochigi|ja|益子町}}, Tochigi Prefecture. It is the festival of {{ill|Yasaka Shrine (Mashiko)|ja|八坂神社}}, which is a subordinate shrine within the grounds of {{ill|Kashima Shrine (Mashiko)|ja|鹿島神社 (益子町)}}<ref>[https://www.town.mashiko.lg.jp/sp/page/page001983.html "Mashiko Sekai Heritage" No.10｜Yasaka Shrine - Mashiko Town Official Website]</ref><ref name="MashikoJouhouKyoku2012_07_24">{{Cite web | url= http://mashikojk.blog.fc2.com/blog-entry-19.html | title= Mashiko's Summer - Gion Festival Departure Ceremony & Handheld Fireworks Dedication | website= Mashiko Information Bureau | date= 2012-7-24 | accessdate=2024-8-1}}</ref><ref name="TouKo_MashikoGionMaturi_2023">{{Cite instagram|user=tokomashiko |author=Tōko / Mashiko Pottery Gallery |postid=CvOdm-FPlD_ |title=Reiwa 5th Year Mashiko Gion Festival was held from July 23 to 25... |date=2023-07-28|access-date=2024-07-26}}</ref><ref name="Mashiko_no_Omatsuri">[http://www.mashiko-kankou.org/culture/traditional_culture.shtml Mashiko Culture - Traditional Culture | Mashiko Tourism Association]</ref><ref name="MashikoGionMatsuri_MashikoSekenIsan_054">{{Cite web | url= https://www.town.mashiko.lg.jp/sp/page/page002973.html | title= Mashiko Sekai Heritage No.54｜Yasaka Shrine Gion Festival (Mashiko Gion Festival / Tenno Festival) | website= Mashiko Town Official Website | date= | accessdate=2024-7-30}}</ref>.
One of the most famous events is the '''"Omiki Choudai-shiki"''' (御神酒頂戴式), a ritual for passing the town's duty, which is considered one of the '''"Three Great Peculiar Festivals of Kanto"''' and is held on the middle day, {{ill|July 24|ja|7月24日}}<ref name=MashikoGionMatsuri_MashikoSekenIsan_054/><ref name=TouKo_MashikoGionMaturi_2023/>{{sfn|Kashiwamura Yūji, Festivals of Tochigi|2012|p=106-108}}.
== Overview == The '''Yasaka Shrine''' in Mashiko is a subordinate shrine of {{ill|Kashima Shrine (Mashiko)|ja|鹿島神社 (益子町)}}<ref name=MashikoGionMatsuri_MashikoSekenIsan_054/>. Every year from '''July 23 to July 25''', the Gion Festival ('''formerly called Tenno Festival''') is held<ref name=MashikoJouhouKyoku2012_07_24/><ref name=MashikoGionMatsuri_MashikoSekenIsan_054/>.
The festival is also known as '''"Yasaka Shrine Gion Festival"'''{{sfn|Kashiwamura Yūji, Festivals of Tochigi|2012|p=106-108}}.
The central town districts of Mashiko, such as '''Shinmachi, Tamachi, Uchimachi, Jōnai, and Dōsodo''', take turns being the hosting town. These five towns, along with '''Hanawa District''', participate with '''festival floats''' that parade through Mashiko’s main streets in a grand celebration{{sfn|Mashiko Exploration, Kuramoto Hidekiyo|1992|p=72}}<ref name=MashikoJouhouKyoku2012_07_24/>.
Many participants are not just original Mashiko residents but also '''potters who have moved to Mashiko''', including '''Takauchi Hideyuki''' and '''Susukida Kōji'''{{sfn|Mashiko Exploration, Kuramoto Hidekiyo|1992|p=72-74}}{{sfn|Shimotsuke Newspaper|1984|p=94-95}}<ref name=SusukidaKouji_no_DeshiTachi>{{Cite instagram|user=ito137.3life |author=ito_susukida |postid=BbiaHMtg5kk |title=Father and his seven apprentices... |date=2017-11-18 |access-date=2023-07-19}}</ref>.
== Origin == Around '''Hōei 2 (1705)''', an '''epidemic''' broke out in Mashiko. To pray for its eradication, the '''Gion belief''' (祇園信仰) originating from Kyoto in the Heian period was introduced{{sfn|Michikake Vol.5, Mashiko Town|2015|p=14}}, and people began '''worshiping Gozu Tennō''', the deity of plagues<ref name=MashikoJouhouKyoku2012_07_24/><ref name=TouKo_MashikoGionMaturi_2023/>{{sfn|Kashiwamura Yūji, Festivals of Tochigi|2012|p=106-108}}.

'''Mashiko Gion Festival''' (益子祇園祭) is a festival held annually from {{ill|July 23|ja|7月23日|qq=draft|qq=draft}} to {{ill|July 25|ja|7月25日|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in {{ill|Mashiko, Tochigi|ja|益子町|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Tochigi Prefecture. It is the festival of {{ill|Yasaka Shrine (Mashiko)|ja|八坂神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, which is a subordinate shrine within the grounds of {{ill|Kashima Shrine (Mashiko)|ja|鹿島神社 (益子町)|qq=draft|qq=draft}}<ref>[https://www.town.mashiko.lg.jp/sp/page/page001983.html "Mashiko Sekai Heritage" No.10｜Yasaka Shrine - Mashiko Town Official Website]</ref><ref name="MashikoJouhouKyoku2012_07_24">{{Cite web | url= http://mashikojk.blog.fc2.com/blog-entry-19.html | title= Mashiko's Summer - Gion Festival Departure Ceremony & Handheld Fireworks Dedication | website= Mashiko Information Bureau | date= 2012-7-24 | accessdate=2024-8-1}}</ref><ref name="TouKo_MashikoGionMaturi_2023">{{Cite instagram|user=tokomashiko |author=Tōko / Mashiko Pottery Gallery |postid=CvOdm-FPlD_ |title=Reiwa 5th Year Mashiko Gion Festival was held from July 23 to 25... |date=2023-07-28|access-date=2024-07-26}}</ref><ref name="Mashiko_no_Omatsuri">[http://www.mashiko-kankou.org/culture/traditional_culture.shtml Mashiko Culture - Traditional Culture | Mashiko Tourism Association]</ref><ref name="MashikoGionMatsuri_MashikoSekenIsan_054">{{Cite web | url= https://www.town.mashiko.lg.jp/sp/page/page002973.html | title= Mashiko Sekai Heritage No.54｜Yasaka Shrine Gion Festival (Mashiko Gion Festival / Tenno Festival) | website= Mashiko Town Official Website | date= | accessdate=2024-7-30}}</ref>.
One of the most famous events is the '''"Omiki Choudai-shiki"''' (御神酒頂戴式), a ritual for passing the town's duty, which is considered one of the '''"Three Great Peculiar Festivals of Kanto"''' and is held on the middle day, {{ill|July 24|ja|7月24日|qq=draft|qq=draft}}<ref name=MashikoGionMatsuri_MashikoSekenIsan_054/><ref name=TouKo_MashikoGionMaturi_2023/>{{sfn|Kashiwamura Yūji, Festivals of Tochigi|2012|p=106-108}}.
== Overview == The '''Yasaka Shrine''' in Mashiko is a subordinate shrine of {{ill|Kashima Shrine (Mashiko)|ja|鹿島神社 (益子町)|qq=draft|qq=draft}}<ref name=MashikoGionMatsuri_MashikoSekenIsan_054/>. Every year from '''July 23 to July 25''', the Gion Festival ('''formerly called Tenno Festival''') is held<ref name=MashikoJouhouKyoku2012_07_24/><ref name=MashikoGionMatsuri_MashikoSekenIsan_054/>.
The festival is also known as '''"Yasaka Shrine Gion Festival"'''{{sfn|Kashiwamura Yūji, Festivals of Tochigi|2012|p=106-108}}.
The central town districts of Mashiko, such as '''Shinmachi, Tamachi, Uchimachi, Jōnai, and Dōsodo''', take turns being the hosting town. These five towns, along with '''Hanawa District''', participate with '''festival floats''' that parade through Mashiko’s main streets in a grand celebration{{sfn|Mashiko Exploration, Kuramoto Hidekiyo|1992|p=72}}<ref name=MashikoJouhouKyoku2012_07_24/>.
Many participants are not just original Mashiko residents but also '''potters who have moved to Mashiko''', including '''Takauchi Hideyuki''' and '''Susukida Kōji'''{{sfn|Mashiko Exploration, Kuramoto Hidekiyo|1992|p=72-74}}{{sfn|Shimotsuke Newspaper|1984|p=94-95}}<ref name=SusukidaKouji_no_DeshiTachi>{{Cite instagram|user=ito137.3life |author=ito_susukida |postid=BbiaHMtg5kk |title=Father and his seven apprentices... |date=2017-11-18 |access-date=2023-07-19}}</ref>.
== Origin == Around '''Hōei 2 (1705)''', an '''epidemic''' broke out in Mashiko. To pray for its eradication, the '''Gion belief''' (祇園信仰) originating from Kyoto in the Heian period was introduced{{sfn|Michikake Vol.5, Mashiko Town|2015|p=14}}, and people began '''worshiping Gozu Tennō''', the deity of plagues<ref name=MashikoJouhouKyoku2012_07_24/><ref name=TouKo_MashikoGionMaturi_2023/>{{sfn|Kashiwamura Yūji, Festivals of Tochigi|2012|p=106-108}}.
---

'''Mashiko Gion Festival''' (益子祇園祭) is a festival held annually from [[:en:July 23|July 23]] to [[:en:July 25|July 25]] in [[:en:Mashiko, Tochigi|Mashiko, Tochigi]], Tochigi Prefecture. It is the festival of [[:en:Yasaka Shrine|Yasaka Shrine (Mashiko)]], which is a subordinate shrine within the grounds of {{ill|Kashima Shrine (Mashiko)|ja|鹿島神社 (益子町)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashima Shrine (Mashiko)|qq=}}<ref>[https://www.town.mashiko.lg.jp/sp/page/page001983.html "Mashiko Sekai Heritage" No.10｜Yasaka Shrine - Mashiko Town Official Website]</ref><ref name="MashikoJouhouKyoku2012_07_24">{{Cite web | url= http://mashikojk.blog.fc2.com/blog-entry-19.html | title= Mashiko's Summer - Gion Festival Departure Ceremony & Handheld Fireworks Dedication | website= Mashiko Information Bureau | date= 2012-7-24 | accessdate=2024-8-1}}</ref><ref name="TouKo_MashikoGionMaturi_2023">{{Cite instagram|user=tokomashiko |author=Tōko / Mashiko Pottery Gallery |postid=CvOdm-FPlD_ |title=Reiwa 5th Year Mashiko Gion Festival was held from July 23 to 25... |date=2023-07-28|access-date=2024-07-26}}</ref><ref name="Mashiko_no_Omatsuri">[http://www.mashiko-kankou.org/culture/traditional_culture.shtml Mashiko Culture - Traditional Culture | Mashiko Tourism Association]</ref><ref name="MashikoGionMatsuri_MashikoSekenIsan_054">{{Cite web | url= https://www.town.mashiko.lg.jp/sp/page/page002973.html | title= Mashiko Sekai Heritage No.54｜Yasaka Shrine Gion Festival (Mashiko Gion Festival / Tenno Festival) | website= Mashiko Town Official Website | date= | accessdate=2024-7-30}}</ref>.
One of the most famous events is the '''"Omiki Choudai-shiki"''' (御神酒頂戴式), a ritual for passing the town's duty, which is considered one of the '''"Three Great Peculiar Festivals of Kanto"''' and is held on the middle day, [[:en:July 24|July 24]]<ref name=MashikoGionMatsuri_MashikoSekenIsan_054/><ref name=TouKo_MashikoGionMaturi_2023/>{{sfn|Kashiwamura Yūji, Festivals of Tochigi|2012|p=106-108}}.
== Overview == The '''Yasaka Shrine''' in Mashiko is a subordinate shrine of {{ill|Kashima Shrine (Mashiko)|ja|鹿島神社 (益子町)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashima Shrine (Mashiko)|qq=}}<ref name=MashikoGionMatsuri_MashikoSekenIsan_054/>. Every year from '''July 23 to July 25''', the Gion Festival ('''formerly called Tenno Festival''') is held<ref name=MashikoJouhouKyoku2012_07_24/><ref name=MashikoGionMatsuri_MashikoSekenIsan_054/>.
The festival is also known as '''"Yasaka Shrine Gion Festival"'''{{sfn|Kashiwamura Yūji, Festivals of Tochigi|2012|p=106-108}}.
The central town districts of Mashiko, such as '''Shinmachi, Tamachi, Uchimachi, Jōnai, and Dōsodo''', take turns being the hosting town. These five towns, along with '''Hanawa District''', participate with '''festival floats''' that parade through Mashiko’s main streets in a grand celebration{{sfn|Mashiko Exploration, Kuramoto Hidekiyo|1992|p=72}}<ref name=MashikoJouhouKyoku2012_07_24/>.
Many participants are not just original Mashiko residents but also '''potters who have moved to Mashiko''', including '''Takauchi Hideyuki''' and '''Susukida Kōji'''{{sfn|Mashiko Exploration, Kuramoto Hidekiyo|1992|p=72-74}}{{sfn|Shimotsuke Newspaper|1984|p=94-95}}<ref name=SusukidaKouji_no_DeshiTachi>{{Cite instagram|user=ito137.3life |author=ito_susukida |postid=BbiaHMtg5kk |title=Father and his seven apprentices... |date=2017-11-18 |access-date=2023-07-19}}</ref>.
== Origin == Around '''Hōei 2 (1705)''', an '''epidemic''' broke out in Mashiko. To pray for its eradication, the '''Gion belief''' (祇園信仰) originating from Kyoto in the Heian period was introduced{{sfn|Michikake Vol.5, Mashiko Town|2015|p=14}}, and people began '''worshiping Gozu Tennō''', the deity of plagues<ref name=MashikoJouhouKyoku2012_07_24/><ref name=TouKo_MashikoGionMaturi_2023/>{{sfn|Kashiwamura Yūji, Festivals of Tochigi|2012|p=106-108}}.
---

The central town districts of Mashiko, such as '''Shinmachi, Tamachi, Uchimachi, Jōnai, and Dōsodo''', take turns being the hosting town. These five towns, along with '''Hanawa District''', participate with '''festival floats''' that parade through Mashiko’s main streets in a grand celebration{{sfn|Mashiko Exploration, Kuramoto Hidekiyo|1992|p=72}}<ref name=MashikoJouhouKyoku2012_07_24/>.
Many participants are not just original Mashiko residents but also '''potters who have moved to Mashiko''', including '''Takauchi Hideyuki''' and '''Susukida Kōji'''{{sfn|Mashiko Exploration, Kuramoto Hidekiyo|1992|p=72-74}}{{sfn|Shimotsuke Newspaper|1984|p=94-95}}<ref name=SusukidaKouji_no_DeshiTachi>{{Cite instagram|user=ito137.3life |author=ito_susukida |postid=BbiaHMtg5kk |title=Father and his seven apprentices... |date=2017-11-18 |access-date=2023-07-19}}</ref>.
== Origin == Around '''Hōei 2 (1705)''', an '''epidemic''' broke out in Mashiko. To pray for its eradication, the '''Gion belief''' (祇園信仰) originating from Kyoto in the Heian period was introduced{{sfn|Michikake Vol.5, Mashiko Town|2015|p=14}}, and people began '''worshiping Gozu Tennō''', the deity of plagues<ref name=MashikoJouhouKyoku2012_07_24/><ref name=TouKo_MashikoGionMaturi_2023/>{{sfn|Kashiwamura Yūji, Festivals of Tochigi|2012|p=106-108}}.

revision.text:
'''Mashiko Gion Festival''' (益子祇園祭) is a festival held annually from [[:en:July 23|July 23]] to [[:en:July 25|July 25]] in [[:en:Mashiko, Tochigi|Mashiko, Tochigi]], Tochigi Prefecture. It is the festival of [[:en:Yasaka Shrine|Yasaka Shrine (Mashiko)]], which is a subordinate shrine within the grounds of {{ill|Kashima Shrine (Mashiko)|ja|鹿島神社 (益子町)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashima Shrine (Mashiko)|qq=}}<ref>[https://www.town.mashiko.lg.jp/sp/page/page001983.html "Mashiko Sekai Heritage" No.10｜Yasaka Shrine - Mashiko Town Official Website]</ref><ref name="MashikoJouhouKyoku2012_07_24">{{Cite web | url= http://mashikojk.blog.fc2.com/blog-entry-19.html | title= Mashiko's Summer - Gion Festival Departure Ceremony & Handheld Fireworks Dedication | website= Mashiko Information Bureau | date= 2012-7-24 | accessdate=2024-8-1}}</ref><ref name="TouKo_MashikoGionMaturi_2023">{{Cite instagram|user=tokomashiko |author=Tōko / Mashiko Pottery Gallery |postid=CvOdm-FPlD_ |title=Reiwa 5th Year Mashiko Gion Festival was held from July 23 to 25... |date=2023-07-28|access-date=2024-07-26}}</ref><ref name="Mashiko_no_Omatsuri">[http://www.mashiko-kankou.org/culture/traditional_culture.shtml Mashiko Culture - Traditional Culture | Mashiko Tourism Association]</ref><ref name="MashikoGionMatsuri_MashikoSekenIsan_054">{{Cite web | url= https://www.town.mashiko.lg.jp/sp/page/page002973.html | title= Mashiko Sekai Heritage No.54｜Yasaka Shrine Gion Festival (Mashiko Gion Festival / Tenno Festival) | website= Mashiko Town Official Website | date= | accessdate=2024-7-30}}</ref>.
One of the most famous events is the '''"Omiki Choudai-shiki"''' (御神酒頂戴式), a ritual for passing the town's duty, which is considered one of the '''"Three Great Peculiar Festivals of Kanto"''' and is held on the middle day, [[:en:July 24|July 24]]<ref name=MashikoGionMatsuri_MashikoSekenIsan_054/><ref name=TouKo_MashikoGionMaturi_2023/>{{sfn|Kashiwamura Yūji, Festivals of Tochigi|2012|p=106-108}}.
== Overview == The '''Yasaka Shrine''' in Mashiko is a subordinate shrine of {{ill|Kashima Shrine (Mashiko)|ja|鹿島神社 (益子町)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashima Shrine (Mashiko)|qq=}}<ref name=MashikoGionMatsuri_MashikoSekenIsan_054/>. Every year from '''July 23 to July 25''', the Gion Festival ('''formerly called Tenno Festival''') is held<ref name=MashikoJouhouKyoku2012_07_24/><ref name=MashikoGionMatsuri_MashikoSekenIsan_054/>.
The festival is also known as '''"Yasaka Shrine Gion Festival"'''{{sfn|Kashiwamura Yūji, Festivals of Tochigi|2012|p=106-108}}.
The central town districts of Mashiko, such as '''Shinmachi, Tamachi, Uchimachi, Jōnai, and Dōsodo''', take turns being the hosting town. These five towns, along with '''Hanawa District''', participate with '''festival floats''' that parade through Mashiko’s main streets in a grand celebration{{sfn|Mashiko Exploration, Kuramoto Hidekiyo|1992|p=72}}<ref name=MashikoJouhouKyoku2012_07_24/>.
Many participants are not just original Mashiko residents but also '''potters who have moved to Mashiko''', including '''Takauchi Hideyuki''' and '''Susukida Kōji'''{{sfn|Mashiko Exploration, Kuramoto Hidekiyo|1992|p=72-74}}{{sfn|Shimotsuke Newspaper|1984|p=94-95}}<ref name=SusukidaKouji_no_DeshiTachi>{{Cite instagram|user=ito137.3life |author=ito_susukida |postid=BbiaHMtg5kk |title=Father and his seven apprentices... |date=2017-11-18 |access-date=2023-07-19}}</ref>.
== Origin == Around '''Hōei 2 (1705)''', an '''epidemic''' broke out in Mashiko. To pray for its eradication, the '''Gion belief''' (祇園信仰) originating from Kyoto in the Heian period was introduced{{sfn|Michikake Vol.5, Mashiko Town|2015|p=14}}, and people began '''worshiping Gozu Tennō''', the deity of plagues<ref name=MashikoJouhouKyoku2012_07_24/><ref name=TouKo_MashikoGionMaturi_2023/>{{sfn|Kashiwamura Yūji, Festivals of Tochigi|2012|p=106-108}}.

revision.comment: Bot: remove deleted category Gion Festivals & Tennō Festivals
revision.text:
'''Mashiko Gion Festival''' (益子祇園祭) is a festival held annually from [[:en:July 23|July 23]] to [[:en:July 25|July 25]] in [[:en:Mashiko, Tochigi|Mashiko, Tochigi]], Tochigi Prefecture. It is the festival of [[:en:Yasaka Shrine|Yasaka Shrine (Mashiko)]], which is a subordinate shrine within the grounds of {{ill|Kashima Shrine (Mashiko)|ja|鹿島神社 (益子町)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashima Shrine (Mashiko)|qq=}}<ref>[https://www.town.mashiko.lg.jp/sp/page/page001983.html "Mashiko Sekai Heritage" No.10｜Yasaka Shrine - Mashiko Town Official Website]</ref><ref name="MashikoJouhouKyoku2012_07_24">{{Cite web | url= http://mashikojk.blog.fc2.com/blog-entry-19.html | title= Mashiko's Summer - Gion Festival Departure Ceremony & Handheld Fireworks Dedication | website= Mashiko Information Bureau | date= 2012-7-24 | accessdate=2024-8-1}}</ref><ref name="TouKo_MashikoGionMaturi_2023">{{Cite instagram|user=tokomashiko |author=Tōko / Mashiko Pottery Gallery |postid=CvOdm-FPlD_ |title=Reiwa 5th Year Mashiko Gion Festival was held from July 23 to 25... |date=2023-07-28|access-date=2024-07-26}}</ref><ref name="Mashiko_no_Omatsuri">[http://www.mashiko-kankou.org/culture/traditional_culture.shtml Mashiko Culture - Traditional Culture | Mashiko Tourism Association]</ref><ref name="MashikoGionMatsuri_MashikoSekenIsan_054">{{Cite web | url= https://www.town.mashiko.lg.jp/sp/page/page002973.html | title= Mashiko Sekai Heritage No.54｜Yasaka Shrine Gion Festival (Mashiko Gion Festival / Tenno Festival) | website= Mashiko Town Official Website | date= | accessdate=2024-7-30}}</ref>.
One of the most famous events is the '''"Omiki Choudai-shiki"''' (御神酒頂戴式), a ritual for passing the town's duty, which is considered one of the '''"Three Great Peculiar Festivals of Kanto"''' and is held on the middle day, [[:en:July 24|July 24]]<ref name=MashikoGionMatsuri_MashikoSekenIsan_054/><ref name=TouKo_MashikoGionMaturi_2023/>{{sfn|Kashiwamura Yūji, Festivals of Tochigi|2012|p=106-108}}.
== Overview == The '''Yasaka Shrine''' in Mashiko is a subordinate shrine of {{ill|Kashima Shrine (Mashiko)|ja|鹿島神社 (益子町)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashima Shrine (Mashiko)|qq=}}<ref name=MashikoGionMatsuri_MashikoSekenIsan_054/>. Every year from '''July 23 to July 25''', the Gion Festival ('''formerly called Tenno Festival''') is held<ref name=MashikoJouhouKyoku2012_07_24/><ref name=MashikoGionMatsuri_MashikoSekenIsan_054/>.
The festival is also known as '''"Yasaka Shrine Gion Festival"'''{{sfn|Kashiwamura Yūji, Festivals of Tochigi|2012|p=106-108}}.
The central town districts of Mashiko, such as '''Shinmachi, Tamachi, Uchimachi, Jōnai, and Dōsodo''', take turns being the hosting town. These five towns, along with '''Hanawa District''', participate with '''festival floats''' that parade through Mashiko’s main streets in a grand celebration{{sfn|Mashiko Exploration, Kuramoto Hidekiyo|1992|p=72}}<ref name=MashikoJouhouKyoku2012_07_24/>.
Many participants are not just original Mashiko residents but also '''potters who have moved to Mashiko''', including '''Takauchi Hideyuki''' and '''Susukida Kōji'''{{sfn|Mashiko Exploration, Kuramoto Hidekiyo|1992|p=72-74}}{{sfn|Shimotsuke Newspaper|1984|p=94-95}}<ref name=SusukidaKouji_no_DeshiTachi>{{Cite instagram|user=ito137.3life |author=ito_susukida |postid=BbiaHMtg5kk |title=Father and his seven apprentices... |date=2017-11-18 |access-date=2023-07-19}}</ref>.
== Origin == Around '''Hōei 2 (1705)''', an '''epidemic''' broke out in Mashiko. To pray for its eradication, the '''Gion belief''' (祇園信仰) originating from Kyoto in the Heian period was introduced{{sfn|Michikake Vol.5, Mashiko Town|2015|p=14}}, and people began '''worshiping Gozu Tennō''', the deity of plagues<ref name=MashikoJouhouKyoku2012_07_24/><ref name=TouKo_MashikoGionMaturi_2023/>{{sfn|Kashiwamura Yūji, Festivals of Tochigi|2012|p=106-108}}.
---
=== '''Category & External Links'''=== * [http://www.mashiko-kankou.org/culture/traditional_culture.shtml Mashiko Culture | Traditional Culture | Gion Festival] * '''Mashiko Tourism Association''' * [https://www.town.mashiko.lg.jp/sp/page/page002973.html Mashiko Sekai Heritage No.54 | Yasaka Shrine Gion Festival] * '''Mashiko Town Official Website''' * [https://tabi-mag.jp/mashiko-gion/ Mashiko Gion Festival] * '''Nippon Tabi Magazine'''

== Overview == The '''Yasaka Shrine''' in Mashiko is a subordinate shrine of {{ill|Kashima Shrine (Mashiko)|ja|鹿島神社 (益子町)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashima Shrine (Mashiko)|qq=}}<ref name=MashikoGionMatsuri_MashikoSekenIsan_054/>. Every year from '''July 23 to July 25''', the Gion Festival ('''formerly called Tenno Festival''') is held<ref name=MashikoJouhouKyoku2012_07_24/><ref name=MashikoGionMatsuri_MashikoSekenIsan_054/>.
The festival is also known as '''"Yasaka Shrine Gion Festival"'''{{sfn|Kashiwamura Yūji, Festivals of Tochigi|2012|p=106-108}}.
The central town districts of Mashiko, such as '''Shinmachi, Tamachi, Uchimachi, Jōnai, and Dōsodo''', take turns being the hosting town. These five towns, along with '''Hanawa District''', participate with '''festival floats''' that parade through Mashiko’s main streets in a grand celebration{{sfn|Mashiko Exploration, Kuramoto Hidekiyo|1992|p=72}}<ref name=MashikoJouhouKyoku2012_07_24/>.
Many participants are not just original Mashiko residents but also '''potters who have moved to Mashiko''', including '''Takauchi Hideyuki''' and '''Susukida Kōji'''{{sfn|Mashiko Exploration, Kuramoto Hidekiyo|1992|p=72-74}}{{sfn|Shimotsuke Newspaper|1984|p=94-95}}<ref name=SusukidaKouji_no_DeshiTachi>{{Cite instagram|user=ito137.3life |author=ito_susukida |postid=BbiaHMtg5kk |title=Father and his seven apprentices... |date=2017-11-18 |access-date=2023-07-19}}</ref>.
== Origin == Around '''Hōei 2 (1705)''', an '''epidemic''' broke out in Mashiko. To pray for its eradication, the '''Gion belief''' (祇園信仰) originating from Kyoto in the Heian period was introduced{{sfn|Michikake Vol.5, Mashiko Town|2015|p=14}}, and people began '''worshiping Gozu Tennō''', the deity of plagues<ref name=MashikoJouhouKyoku2012_07_24/><ref name=TouKo_MashikoGionMaturi_2023/>{{sfn|Kashiwamura Yūji, Festivals of Tochigi|2012|p=106-108}}.
---
