revision.text:
'''Mashiko Gion Festival''' (益子祇園祭) is a festival held annually from [[:en:July 23|July 23]] to [[:en:July 25|July 25]] in [[:en:Mashiko, Tochigi|Mashiko, Tochigi]], Tochigi Prefecture. It is the festival of [[:en:Yasaka Shrine|Yasaka Shrine (Mashiko)]], which is a subordinate shrine within the grounds of {{ill|Kashima Shrine (Mashiko)|ja|鹿島神社 (益子町)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashima Shrine (Mashiko)|qq=}}<ref>[https://www.town.mashiko.lg.jp/sp/page/page001983.html "Mashiko Sekai Heritage" No.10｜Yasaka Shrine - Mashiko Town Official Website]</ref><ref name="MashikoJouhouKyoku2012_07_24">{{Cite web | url= http://mashikojk.blog.fc2.com/blog-entry-19.html | title= Mashiko's Summer - Gion Festival Departure Ceremony & Handheld Fireworks Dedication | website= Mashiko Information Bureau | date= 2012-7-24 | accessdate=2024-8-1}}</ref><ref name="TouKo_MashikoGionMaturi_2023">{{Cite instagram|user=tokomashiko |author=Tōko / Mashiko Pottery Gallery |postid=CvOdm-FPlD_ |title=Reiwa 5th Year Mashiko Gion Festival was held from July 23 to 25... |date=2023-07-28|access-date=2024-07-26}}</ref><ref name="Mashiko_no_Omatsuri">[http://www.mashiko-kankou.org/culture/traditional_culture.shtml Mashiko Culture - Traditional Culture | Mashiko Tourism Association]</ref><ref name="MashikoGionMatsuri_MashikoSekenIsan_054">{{Cite web | url= https://www.town.mashiko.lg.jp/sp/page/page002973.html | title= Mashiko Sekai Heritage No.54｜Yasaka Shrine Gion Festival (Mashiko Gion Festival / Tenno Festival) | website= Mashiko Town Official Website | date= | accessdate=2024-7-30}}</ref>.
One of the most famous events is the '''"Omiki Choudai-shiki"''' (御神酒頂戴式), a ritual for passing the town's duty, which is considered one of the '''"Three Great Peculiar Festivals of Kanto"''' and is held on the middle day, [[:en:July 24|July 24]]<ref name=MashikoGionMatsuri_MashikoSekenIsan_054/><ref name=TouKo_MashikoGionMaturi_2023/>{{sfn|Kashiwamura Yūji, Festivals of Tochigi|2012|p=106-108}}.
== Overview == The '''Yasaka Shrine''' in Mashiko is a subordinate shrine of {{ill|Kashima Shrine (Mashiko)|ja|鹿島神社 (益子町)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashima Shrine (Mashiko)|qq=}}<ref name=MashikoGionMatsuri_MashikoSekenIsan_054/>. Every year from '''July 23 to July 25''', the Gion Festival ('''formerly called Tenno Festival''') is held<ref name=MashikoJouhouKyoku2012_07_24/><ref name=MashikoGionMatsuri_MashikoSekenIsan_054/>.
The festival is also known as '''"Yasaka Shrine Gion Festival"'''{{sfn|Kashiwamura Yūji, Festivals of Tochigi|2012|p=106-108}}.
The central town districts of Mashiko, such as '''Shinmachi, Tamachi, Uchimachi, Jōnai, and Dōsodo''', take turns being the hosting town. These five towns, along with '''Hanawa District''', participate with '''festival floats''' that parade through Mashiko’s main streets in a grand celebration{{sfn|Mashiko Exploration, Kuramoto Hidekiyo|1992|p=72}}<ref name=MashikoJouhouKyoku2012_07_24/>.
Many participants are not just original Mashiko residents but also '''potters who have moved to Mashiko''', including '''Takauchi Hideyuki''' and '''Susukida Kōji'''{{sfn|Mashiko Exploration, Kuramoto Hidekiyo|1992|p=72-74}}{{sfn|Shimotsuke Newspaper|1984|p=94-95}}<ref name=SusukidaKouji_no_DeshiTachi>{{Cite instagram|user=ito137.3life |author=ito_susukida |postid=BbiaHMtg5kk |title=Father and his seven apprentices... |date=2017-11-18 |access-date=2023-07-19}}</ref>.
== Origin == Around '''Hōei 2 (1705)''', an '''epidemic''' broke out in Mashiko. To pray for its eradication, the '''Gion belief''' (祇園信仰) originating from Kyoto in the Heian period was introduced{{sfn|Michikake Vol.5, Mashiko Town|2015|p=14}}, and people began '''worshiping Gozu Tennō''', the deity of plagues<ref name=MashikoJouhouKyoku2012_07_24/><ref name=TouKo_MashikoGionMaturi_2023/>{{sfn|Kashiwamura Yūji, Festivals of Tochigi|2012|p=106-108}}.
---

revision.text:
{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11581191]] Wikidata name: Mashiko Gion Matsuri [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:20, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:03, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Mashiko Gion Festival''' (益子祇園祭) is a festival held annually from [[:en:July 23|July 23]] to [[:en:July 25|July 25]] in [[:en:Mashiko, Tochigi|Mashiko, Tochigi]], Tochigi Prefecture. It is the festival of [[:en:Yasaka Shrine|Yasaka Shrine (Mashiko)]], which is a subordinate shrine within the grounds of {{ill|Kashima Shrine (Mashiko)|ja|鹿島神社 (益子町)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashima Shrine (Mashiko)|qq=}}<ref>[https://www.town.mashiko.lg.jp/sp/page/page001983.html "Mashiko Sekai Heritage" No.10｜Yasaka Shrine - Mashiko Town Official Website]</ref><ref name="MashikoJouhouKyoku2012_07_24">{{Cite web | url= http://mashikojk.blog.fc2.com/blog-entry-19.html | title= Mashiko's Summer - Gion Festival Departure Ceremony & Handheld Fireworks Dedication | website= Mashiko Information Bureau | date= 2012-7-24 | accessdate=2024-8-1}}</ref><ref name="TouKo_MashikoGionMaturi_2023">{{Cite instagram|user=tokomashiko |author=Tōko / Mashiko Pottery Gallery |postid=CvOdm-FPlD_ |title=Reiwa 5th Year Mashiko Gion Festival was held from July 23 to 25... |date=2023-07-28|access-date=2024-07-26}}</ref><ref name="Mashiko_no_Omatsuri">[http://www.mashiko-kankou.org/culture/traditional_culture.shtml Mashiko Culture - Traditional Culture | Mashiko Tourism Association]</ref><ref name="MashikoGionMatsuri_MashikoSekenIsan_054">{{Cite web | url= https://www.town.mashiko.lg.jp/sp/page/page002973.html | title= Mashiko Sekai Heritage No.54｜Yasaka Shrine Gion Festival (Mashiko Gion Festival / Tenno Festival) | website= Mashiko Town Official Website | date= | accessdate=2024-7-30}}</ref>.
One of the most famous events is the '''"Omiki Choudai-shiki"''' (御神酒頂戴式), a ritual for passing the town's duty, which is considered one of the '''"Three Great Peculiar Festivals of Kanto"''' and is held on the middle day, [[:en:July 24|July 24]]<ref name=MashikoGionMatsuri_MashikoSekenIsan_054/><ref name=TouKo_MashikoGionMaturi_2023/>{{sfn|Kashiwamura Yūji, Festivals of Tochigi|2012|p=106-108}}.
== Overview == The '''Yasaka Shrine''' in Mashiko is a subordinate shrine of {{ill|Kashima Shrine (Mashiko)|ja|鹿島神社 (益子町)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashima Shrine (Mashiko)|qq=}}<ref name=MashikoGionMatsuri_MashikoSekenIsan_054/>. Every year from '''July 23 to July 25''', the Gion Festival ('''formerly called Tenno Festival''') is held<ref name=MashikoJouhouKyoku2012_07_24/><ref name=MashikoGionMatsuri_MashikoSekenIsan_054/>.
The festival is also known as '''"Yasaka Shrine Gion Festival"'''{{sfn|Kashiwamura Yūji, Festivals of Tochigi|2012|p=106-108}}.
The central town districts of Mashiko, such as '''Shinmachi, Tamachi, Uchimachi, Jōnai, and Dōsodo''', take turns being the hosting town. These five towns, along with '''Hanawa District''', participate with '''festival floats''' that parade through Mashiko’s main streets in a grand celebration{{sfn|Mashiko Exploration, Kuramoto Hidekiyo|1992|p=72}}<ref name=MashikoJouhouKyoku2012_07_24/>.
Many participants are not just original Mashiko residents but also '''potters who have moved to Mashiko''', including '''Takauchi Hideyuki''' and '''Susukida Kōji'''{{sfn|Mashiko Exploration, Kuramoto Hidekiyo|1992|p=72-74}}{{sfn|Shimotsuke Newspaper|1984|p=94-95}}<ref name=SusukidaKouji_no_DeshiTachi>{{Cite instagram|user=ito137.3life |author=ito_susukida |postid=BbiaHMtg5kk |title=Father and his seven apprentices... |date=2017-11-18 |access-date=2023-07-19}}</ref>.
== Origin == Around '''Hōei 2 (1705)''', an '''epidemic''' broke out in Mashiko. To pray for its eradication, the '''Gion belief''' (祇園信仰) originating from Kyoto in the Heian period was introduced{{sfn|Michikake Vol.5, Mashiko Town|2015|p=14}}, and people began '''worshiping Gozu Tennō''', the deity of plagues<ref name=MashikoJouhouKyoku2012_07_24/><ref name=TouKo_MashikoGionMaturi_2023/>{{sfn|Kashiwamura Yūji, Festivals of Tochigi|2012|p=106-108}}.
---

<!--Mashiko Gion Festival--> [[Category:Festivals in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:Mashiko, Tochigi]] [[Category:History of Mashiko Town]] [[Category:Gion festivals]] <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> [[ja:益子祇園祭]]

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11581191]] Wikidata name: Mashiko Gion Matsuri [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:20, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:03, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Mashiko Gion Festival''' (益子祇園祭) is a festival held annually from [[:en:July 23|July 23]] to [[:en:July 25|July 25]] in [[:en:Mashiko, Tochigi|Mashiko, Tochigi]], Tochigi Prefecture. It is the festival of [[:en:Yasaka Shrine|Yasaka Shrine (Mashiko)]], which is a subordinate shrine within the grounds of {{ill|Kashima Shrine (Mashiko)|ja|鹿島神社 (益子町)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashima Shrine (Mashiko)|qq=|1=Kashima Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q134891806}}<ref>[https://www.town.mashiko.lg.jp/sp/page/page001983.html "Mashiko Sekai Heritage" No.10｜Yasaka Shrine - Mashiko Town Official Website]</ref><ref name="MashikoJouhouKyoku2012_07_24">{{Cite web | url= http://mashikojk.blog.fc2.com/blog-entry-19.html | title= Mashiko's Summer - Gion Festival Departure Ceremony & Handheld Fireworks Dedication | website= Mashiko Information Bureau | date= 2012-7-24 | accessdate=2024-8-1}}</ref><ref name="TouKo_MashikoGionMaturi_2023">{{Cite instagram|user=tokomashiko |author=Tōko / Mashiko Pottery Gallery |postid=CvOdm-FPlD_ |title=Reiwa 5th Year Mashiko Gion Festival was held from July 23 to 25... |date=2023-07-28|access-date=2024-07-26}}</ref><ref name="Mashiko_no_Omatsuri">[http://www.mashiko-kankou.org/culture/traditional_culture.shtml Mashiko Culture - Traditional Culture | Mashiko Tourism Association]</ref><ref name="MashikoGionMatsuri_MashikoSekenIsan_054">{{Cite web | url= https://www.town.mashiko.lg.jp/sp/page/page002973.html | title= Mashiko Sekai Heritage No.54｜Yasaka Shrine Gion Festival (Mashiko Gion Festival / Tenno Festival) | website= Mashiko Town Official Website | date= | accessdate=2024-7-30}}</ref>.
One of the most famous events is the '''"Omiki Choudai-shiki"''' (御神酒頂戴式), a ritual for passing the town's duty, which is considered one of the '''"Three Great Peculiar Festivals of Kanto"''' and is held on the middle day, [[:en:July 24|July 24]]<ref name=MashikoGionMatsuri_MashikoSekenIsan_054/><ref name=TouKo_MashikoGionMaturi_2023/>{{sfn|Kashiwamura Yūji, Festivals of Tochigi|2012|p=106-108}}.
== Overview == The '''Yasaka Shrine''' in Mashiko is a subordinate shrine of {{ill|Kashima Shrine (Mashiko)|ja|鹿島神社 (益子町)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashima Shrine (Mashiko)|qq=|1=Kashima Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q134891806}}<ref name=MashikoGionMatsuri_MashikoSekenIsan_054/>. Every year from '''July 23 to July 25''', the Gion Festival ('''formerly called Tenno Festival''') is held<ref name=MashikoJouhouKyoku2012_07_24/><ref name=MashikoGionMatsuri_MashikoSekenIsan_054/>.
The festival is also known as '''"Yasaka Shrine Gion Festival"'''{{sfn|Kashiwamura Yūji, Festivals of Tochigi|2012|p=106-108}}.
The central town districts of Mashiko, such as '''Shinmachi, Tamachi, Uchimachi, Jōnai, and Dōsodo''', take turns being the hosting town. These five towns, along with '''Hanawa District''', participate with '''festival floats''' that parade through Mashiko’s main streets in a grand celebration{{sfn|Mashiko Exploration, Kuramoto Hidekiyo|1992|p=72}}<ref name=MashikoJouhouKyoku2012_07_24/>.
Many participants are not just original Mashiko residents but also '''potters who have moved to Mashiko''', including '''Takauchi Hideyuki''' and '''Susukida Kōji'''{{sfn|Mashiko Exploration, Kuramoto Hidekiyo|1992|p=72-74}}{{sfn|Shimotsuke Newspaper|1984|p=94-95}}<ref name=SusukidaKouji_no_DeshiTachi>{{Cite instagram|user=ito137.3life |author=ito_susukida |postid=BbiaHMtg5kk |title=Father and his seven apprentices... |date=2017-11-18 |access-date=2023-07-19}}</ref>.
== Origin == Around '''Hōei 2 (1705)''', an '''epidemic''' broke out in Mashiko. To pray for its eradication, the '''Gion belief''' (祇園信仰) originating from Kyoto in the Heian period was introduced{{sfn|Michikake Vol.5, Mashiko Town|2015|p=14}}, and people began '''worshiping Gozu Tennō''', the deity of plagues<ref name=MashikoJouhouKyoku2012_07_24/><ref name=TouKo_MashikoGionMaturi_2023/>{{sfn|Kashiwamura Yūji, Festivals of Tochigi|2012|p=106-108}}.
---

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:益子祇園祭]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11581191]] Wikidata name: Mashiko Gion Matsuri [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:20, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:03, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Mashiko Gion Festival''' (益子祇園祭) is a festival held annually from [[:en:July 23|July 23]] to [[:en:July 25|July 25]] in [[:en:Mashiko, Tochigi|Mashiko, Tochigi]], Tochigi Prefecture. It is the festival of [[:en:Yasaka Shrine|Yasaka Shrine (Mashiko)]], which is a subordinate shrine within the grounds of {{ill|Kashima Shrine (Mashiko)|ja|鹿島神社 (益子町)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashima Shrine (Mashiko)|1=Kashima Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q134891806}}<ref>[https://www.town.mashiko.lg.jp/sp/page/page001983.html "Mashiko Sekai Heritage" No.10｜Yasaka Shrine - Mashiko Town Official Website]</ref><ref name="MashikoJouhouKyoku2012_07_24">{{Cite web | url= http://mashikojk.blog.fc2.com/blog-entry-19.html | title= Mashiko's Summer - Gion Festival Departure Ceremony & Handheld Fireworks Dedication | website= Mashiko Information Bureau | date= 2012-7-24 | accessdate=2024-8-1}}</ref><ref name="TouKo_MashikoGionMaturi_2023">{{Cite instagram|user=tokomashiko |author=Tōko / Mashiko Pottery Gallery |postid=CvOdm-FPlD_ |title=Reiwa 5th Year Mashiko Gion Festival was held from July 23 to 25... |date=2023-07-28|access-date=2024-07-26}}</ref><ref name="Mashiko_no_Omatsuri">[http://www.mashiko-kankou.org/culture/traditional_culture.shtml Mashiko Culture - Traditional Culture | Mashiko Tourism Association]</ref><ref name="MashikoGionMatsuri_MashikoSekenIsan_054">{{Cite web | url= https://www.town.mashiko.lg.jp/sp/page/page002973.html | title= Mashiko Sekai Heritage No.54｜Yasaka Shrine Gion Festival (Mashiko Gion Festival / Tenno Festival) | website= Mashiko Town Official Website | date= | accessdate=2024-7-30}}</ref>.
One of the most famous events is the '''"Omiki Choudai-shiki"''' (御神酒頂戴式), a ritual for passing the town's duty, which is considered one of the '''"Three Great Peculiar Festivals of Kanto"''' and is held on the middle day, [[:en:July 24|July 24]]<ref name=MashikoGionMatsuri_MashikoSekenIsan_054/><ref name=TouKo_MashikoGionMaturi_2023/>{{sfn|Kashiwamura Yūji, Festivals of Tochigi|2012|p=106-108}}.
== Overview == The '''Yasaka Shrine''' in Mashiko is a subordinate shrine of {{ill|Kashima Shrine (Mashiko)|ja|鹿島神社 (益子町)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashima Shrine (Mashiko)|1=Kashima Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q134891806}}<ref name=MashikoGionMatsuri_MashikoSekenIsan_054/>. Every year from '''July 23 to July 25''', the Gion Festival ('''formerly called Tenno Festival''') is held<ref name=MashikoJouhouKyoku2012_07_24/><ref name=MashikoGionMatsuri_MashikoSekenIsan_054/>.
The festival is also known as '''"Yasaka Shrine Gion Festival"'''{{sfn|Kashiwamura Yūji, Festivals of Tochigi|2012|p=106-108}}.
The central town districts of Mashiko, such as '''Shinmachi, Tamachi, Uchimachi, Jōnai, and Dōsodo''', take turns being the hosting town. These five towns, along with '''Hanawa District''', participate with '''festival floats''' that parade through Mashiko’s main streets in a grand celebration{{sfn|Mashiko Exploration, Kuramoto Hidekiyo|1992|p=72}}<ref name=MashikoJouhouKyoku2012_07_24/>.
Many participants are not just original Mashiko residents but also '''potters who have moved to Mashiko''', including '''Takauchi Hideyuki''' and '''Susukida Kōji'''{{sfn|Mashiko Exploration, Kuramoto Hidekiyo|1992|p=72-74}}{{sfn|Shimotsuke Newspaper|1984|p=94-95}}<ref name=SusukidaKouji_no_DeshiTachi>{{Cite instagram|user=ito137.3life |author=ito_susukida |postid=BbiaHMtg5kk |title=Father and his seven apprentices... |date=2017-11-18 |access-date=2023-07-19}}</ref>.
== Origin == Around '''Hōei 2 (1705)''', an '''epidemic''' broke out in Mashiko. To pray for its eradication, the '''Gion belief''' (祇園信仰) originating from Kyoto in the Heian period was introduced{{sfn|Michikake Vol.5, Mashiko Town|2015|p=14}}, and people began '''worshiping Gozu Tennō''', the deity of plagues<ref name=MashikoJouhouKyoku2012_07_24/><ref name=TouKo_MashikoGionMaturi_2023/>{{sfn|Kashiwamura Yūji, Festivals of Tochigi|2012|p=106-108}}.
---

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:益子祇園祭]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11581191]] Wikidata name: Mashiko Gion Matsuri [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:20, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:03, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Mashiko Gion Festival''' (益子祇園祭) is a festival held annually from [[:en:July 23|July 23]] to [[:en:July 25|July 25]] in [[:en:Mashiko, Tochigi|Mashiko, Tochigi]], Tochigi Prefecture. It is the festival of [[:en:Yasaka Shrine|Yasaka Shrine (Mashiko)]], which is a subordinate shrine within the grounds of {{ill|Kashima Shrine (Mashiko)|ja|鹿島神社 (益子町)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashima Shrine (Mashiko)|1=Kashima Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q134891806}}<ref>[https://www.town.mashiko.lg.jp/sp/page/page001983.html "Mashiko Sekai Heritage" No.10｜Yasaka Shrine - Mashiko Town Official Website]</ref><ref name="MashikoJouhouKyoku2012_07_24">{{Cite web | url= http://mashikojk.blog.fc2.com/blog-entry-19.html | title= Mashiko's Summer - Gion Festival Departure Ceremony & Handheld Fireworks Dedication | website= Mashiko Information Bureau | date= 2012-7-24 | accessdate=2024-8-1}}</ref><ref name="TouKo_MashikoGionMaturi_2023">{{Cite instagram|user=tokomashiko |author=Tōko / Mashiko Pottery Gallery |postid=CvOdm-FPlD_ |title=Reiwa 5th Year Mashiko Gion Festival was held from July 23 to 25... |date=2023-07-28|access-date=2024-07-26}}</ref><ref name="Mashiko_no_Omatsuri">[http://www.mashiko-kankou.org/culture/traditional_culture.shtml Mashiko Culture - Traditional Culture | Mashiko Tourism Association]</ref><ref name="MashikoGionMatsuri_MashikoSekenIsan_054">{{Cite web | url= https://www.town.mashiko.lg.jp/sp/page/page002973.html | title= Mashiko Sekai Heritage No.54｜Yasaka Shrine Gion Festival (Mashiko Gion Festival / Tenno Festival) | website= Mashiko Town Official Website | date= | accessdate=2024-7-30}}</ref>.
One of the most famous events is the '''"Omiki Choudai-shiki"''' (御神酒頂戴式), a ritual for passing the town's duty, which is considered one of the '''"Three Great Peculiar Festivals of Kanto"''' and is held on the middle day, [[:en:July 24|July 24]]<ref name=MashikoGionMatsuri_MashikoSekenIsan_054/><ref name=TouKo_MashikoGionMaturi_2023/>{{sfn|Kashiwamura Yūji, Festivals of Tochigi|2012|p=106-108}}.
== Overview == The '''Yasaka Shrine''' in Mashiko is a subordinate shrine of {{ill|Kashima Shrine (Mashiko)|ja|鹿島神社 (益子町)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashima Shrine (Mashiko)|1=Kashima Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q134891806}}<ref name=MashikoGionMatsuri_MashikoSekenIsan_054/>. Every year from '''July 23 to July 25''', the Gion Festival ('''formerly called Tenno Festival''') is held<ref name=MashikoJouhouKyoku2012_07_24/><ref name=MashikoGionMatsuri_MashikoSekenIsan_054/>.
The festival is also known as '''"Yasaka Shrine Gion Festival"'''{{sfn|Kashiwamura Yūji, Festivals of Tochigi|2012|p=106-108}}.
The central town districts of Mashiko, such as '''Shinmachi, Tamachi, Uchimachi, Jōnai, and Dōsodo''', take turns being the hosting town. These five towns, along with '''Hanawa District''', participate with '''festival floats''' that parade through Mashiko’s main streets in a grand celebration{{sfn|Mashiko Exploration, Kuramoto Hidekiyo|1992|p=72}}<ref name=MashikoJouhouKyoku2012_07_24/>.
Many participants are not just original Mashiko residents but also '''potters who have moved to Mashiko''', including '''Takauchi Hideyuki''' and '''Susukida Kōji'''{{sfn|Mashiko Exploration, Kuramoto Hidekiyo|1992|p=72-74}}{{sfn|Shimotsuke Newspaper|1984|p=94-95}}<ref name=SusukidaKouji_no_DeshiTachi>{{Cite instagram|user=ito137.3life |author=ito_susukida |postid=BbiaHMtg5kk |title=Father and his seven apprentices... |date=2017-11-18 |access-date=2023-07-19}}</ref>.
== Origin == Around '''Hōei 2 (1705)''', an '''epidemic''' broke out in Mashiko. To pray for its eradication, the '''Gion belief''' (祇園信仰) originating from Kyoto in the Heian period was introduced{{sfn|Michikake Vol.5, Mashiko Town|2015|p=14}}, and people began '''worshiping Gozu Tennō''', the deity of plagues<ref name=MashikoJouhouKyoku2012_07_24/><ref name=TouKo_MashikoGionMaturi_2023/>{{sfn|Kashiwamura Yūji, Festivals of Tochigi|2012|p=106-108}}.
---

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:益子祇園祭]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11581191]] Wikidata name: Mashiko Gion Matsuri [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:20, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:03, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Mashiko Gion Festival''' (益子祇園祭) is a festival held annually from [[:en:July 23|July 23]] to [[:en:July 25|July 25]] in [[:en:Mashiko, Tochigi|Mashiko, Tochigi]], Tochigi Prefecture. It is the festival of [[:en:Yasaka Shrine|Yasaka Shrine (Mashiko)]], which is a subordinate shrine within the grounds of {{ill|Kashima Shrine (Mashiko)|ja|鹿島神社 (益子町)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashima Shrine (Mashiko)|1=Kashima Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q134891806}}<ref>[https://www.town.mashiko.lg.jp/sp/page/page001983.html "Mashiko Sekai Heritage" No.10｜Yasaka Shrine - Mashiko Town Official Website]</ref><ref name="MashikoJouhouKyoku2012_07_24">{{Cite web | url= http://mashikojk.blog.fc2.com/blog-entry-19.html | title= Mashiko's Summer - Gion Festival Departure Ceremony & Handheld Fireworks Dedication | website= Mashiko Information Bureau | date= 2012-7-24 | accessdate=2024-8-1}}</ref><ref name="TouKo_MashikoGionMaturi_2023">{{Cite instagram|user=tokomashiko |author=Tōko / Mashiko Pottery Gallery |postid=CvOdm-FPlD_ |title=Reiwa 5th Year Mashiko Gion Festival was held from July 23 to 25... |date=2023-07-28|access-date=2024-07-26}}</ref><ref name="Mashiko_no_Omatsuri">[http://www.mashiko-kankou.org/culture/traditional_culture.shtml Mashiko Culture - Traditional Culture | Mashiko Tourism Association]</ref><ref name="MashikoGionMatsuri_MashikoSekenIsan_054">{{Cite web | url= https://www.town.mashiko.lg.jp/sp/page/page002973.html | title= Mashiko Sekai Heritage No.54｜Yasaka Shrine Gion Festival (Mashiko Gion Festival / Tenno Festival) | website= Mashiko Town Official Website | date= | accessdate=2024-7-30}}</ref>.
One of the most famous events is the '''"Omiki Choudai-shiki"''' (御神酒頂戴式), a ritual for passing the town's duty, which is considered one of the '''"Three Great Peculiar Festivals of Kanto"''' and is held on the middle day, [[:en:July 24|July 24]]<ref name=MashikoGionMatsuri_MashikoSekenIsan_054/><ref name=TouKo_MashikoGionMaturi_2023/>{{sfn|Kashiwamura Yūji, Festivals of Tochigi|2012|p=106-108}}.
== Overview == The '''Yasaka Shrine''' in Mashiko is a subordinate shrine of {{ill|Kashima Shrine (Mashiko)|ja|鹿島神社 (益子町)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashima Shrine (Mashiko)|1=Kashima Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q134891806}}<ref name=MashikoGionMatsuri_MashikoSekenIsan_054/>. Every year from '''July 23 to July 25''', the Gion Festival ('''formerly called Tenno Festival''') is held<ref name=MashikoJouhouKyoku2012_07_24/><ref name=MashikoGionMatsuri_MashikoSekenIsan_054/>.
The festival is also known as '''"Yasaka Shrine Gion Festival"'''{{sfn|Kashiwamura Yūji, Festivals of Tochigi|2012|p=106-108}}.
The central town districts of Mashiko, such as '''Shinmachi, Tamachi, Uchimachi, Jōnai, and Dōsodo''', take turns being the hosting town. These five towns, along with '''Hanawa District''', participate with '''festival floats''' that parade through Mashiko’s main streets in a grand celebration{{sfn|Mashiko Exploration, Kuramoto Hidekiyo|1992|p=72}}<ref name=MashikoJouhouKyoku2012_07_24/>.
Many participants are not just original Mashiko residents but also '''potters who have moved to Mashiko''', including '''Takauchi Hideyuki''' and '''Susukida Kōji'''{{sfn|Mashiko Exploration, Kuramoto Hidekiyo|1992|p=72-74}}{{sfn|Shimotsuke Newspaper|1984|p=94-95}}<ref name=SusukidaKouji_no_DeshiTachi>{{Cite instagram|user=ito137.3life |author=ito_susukida |postid=BbiaHMtg5kk |title=Father and his seven apprentices... |date=2017-11-18 |access-date=2023-07-19}}</ref>.
== Origin == Around '''Hōei 2 (1705)''', an '''epidemic''' broke out in Mashiko. To pray for its eradication, the '''Gion belief''' (祇園信仰) originating from Kyoto in the Heian period was introduced{{sfn|Michikake Vol.5, Mashiko Town|2015|p=14}}, and people began '''worshiping Gozu Tennō''', the deity of plagues<ref name=MashikoJouhouKyoku2012_07_24/><ref name=TouKo_MashikoGionMaturi_2023/>{{sfn|Kashiwamura Yūji, Festivals of Tochigi|2012|p=106-108}}.
---
=== '''Category & External Links'''=== * [http://www.mashiko-kankou.org/culture/traditional_culture.shtml Mashiko Culture | Traditional Culture | Gion Festival] * '''Mashiko Tourism Association''' * [https://www.town.mashiko.lg.jp/sp/page/page002973.html Mashiko Sekai Heritage No.54 | Yasaka Shrine Gion Festival] * '''Mashiko Town Official Website''' * [https://tabi-mag.jp/mashiko-gion/ Mashiko Gion Festival] * '''Nippon Tabi Magazine'''
{{translated page|ja|益子祇園祭|version=102298556|comment=Imported full ja history}}
{{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 6]]
}}

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:益子祇園祭]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:益子祇園祭]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:益子祇園祭]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11581191]] Wikidata name: Mashiko Gion Matsuri [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:20, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:03, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Mashiko Gion Festival''' (益子祇園祭) is a festival held annually from [[:en:July 23|July 23]] to [[:en:July 25|July 25]] in [[:en:Mashiko, Tochigi|Mashiko, Tochigi]], Tochigi Prefecture. It is the festival of [[:en:Yasaka Shrine|Yasaka Shrine (Mashiko)]], which is a subordinate shrine within the grounds of {{ill|Kashima Shrine (Mashiko)|ja|鹿島神社 (益子町)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashima Shrine (Mashiko)|1=Kashima Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q134891806}}<ref>[https://www.town.mashiko.lg.jp/sp/page/page001983.html "Mashiko Sekai Heritage" No.10｜Yasaka Shrine - Mashiko Town Official Website]</ref><ref name="MashikoJouhouKyoku2012_07_24">{{Cite web | url= http://mashikojk.blog.fc2.com/blog-entry-19.html | title= Mashiko's Summer - Gion Festival Departure Ceremony & Handheld Fireworks Dedication | website= Mashiko Information Bureau | date= 2012-7-24 | accessdate=2024-8-1}}</ref><ref name="TouKo_MashikoGionMaturi_2023">{{Cite instagram|user=tokomashiko |author=Tōko / Mashiko Pottery Gallery |postid=CvOdm-FPlD_ |title=Reiwa 5th Year Mashiko Gion Festival was held from July 23 to 25... |date=2023-07-28|access-date=2024-07-26}}</ref><ref name="Mashiko_no_Omatsuri">[http://www.mashiko-kankou.org/culture/traditional_culture.shtml Mashiko Culture - Traditional Culture | Mashiko Tourism Association]</ref><ref name="MashikoGionMatsuri_MashikoSekenIsan_054">{{Cite web | url= https://www.town.mashiko.lg.jp/sp/page/page002973.html | title= Mashiko Sekai Heritage No.54｜Yasaka Shrine Gion Festival (Mashiko Gion Festival / Tenno Festival) | website= Mashiko Town Official Website | date= | accessdate=2024-7-30}}</ref>.
One of the most famous events is the '''"Omiki Choudai-shiki"''' (御神酒頂戴式), a ritual for passing the town's duty, which is considered one of the '''"Three Great Peculiar Festivals of Kanto"''' and is held on the middle day, [[:en:July 24|July 24]]<ref name=MashikoGionMatsuri_MashikoSekenIsan_054/><ref name=TouKo_MashikoGionMaturi_2023/>{{sfn|Kashiwamura Yūji, Festivals of Tochigi|2012|p=106-108}}.
== Overview == The '''Yasaka Shrine''' in Mashiko is a subordinate shrine of {{ill|Kashima Shrine (Mashiko)|ja|鹿島神社 (益子町)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashima Shrine (Mashiko)|1=Kashima Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q134891806}}<ref name=MashikoGionMatsuri_MashikoSekenIsan_054/>. Every year from '''July 23 to July 25''', the Gion Festival ('''formerly called Tenno Festival''') is held<ref name=MashikoJouhouKyoku2012_07_24/><ref name=MashikoGionMatsuri_MashikoSekenIsan_054/>.
The festival is also known as '''"Yasaka Shrine Gion Festival"'''{{sfn|Kashiwamura Yūji, Festivals of Tochigi|2012|p=106-108}}.
The central town districts of Mashiko, such as '''Shinmachi, Tamachi, Uchimachi, Jōnai, and Dōsodo''', take turns being the hosting town. These five towns, along with '''Hanawa District''', participate with '''festival floats''' that parade through Mashiko’s main streets in a grand celebration{{sfn|Mashiko Exploration, Kuramoto Hidekiyo|1992|p=72}}<ref name=MashikoJouhouKyoku2012_07_24/>.
Many participants are not just original Mashiko residents but also '''potters who have moved to Mashiko''', including '''Takauchi Hideyuki''' and '''Susukida Kōji'''{{sfn|Mashiko Exploration, Kuramoto Hidekiyo|1992|p=72-74}}{{sfn|Shimotsuke Newspaper|1984|p=94-95}}<ref name=SusukidaKouji_no_DeshiTachi>{{Cite instagram|user=ito137.3life |author=ito_susukida |postid=BbiaHMtg5kk |title=Father and his seven apprentices... |date=2017-11-18 |access-date=2023-07-19}}</ref>.
== Origin == Around '''Hōei 2 (1705)''', an '''epidemic''' broke out in Mashiko. To pray for its eradication, the '''Gion belief''' (祇園信仰) originating from Kyoto in the Heian period was introduced{{sfn|Michikake Vol.5, Mashiko Town|2015|p=14}}, and people began '''worshiping Gozu Tennō''', the deity of plagues<ref name=MashikoJouhouKyoku2012_07_24/><ref name=TouKo_MashikoGionMaturi_2023/>{{sfn|Kashiwamura Yūji, Festivals of Tochigi|2012|p=106-108}}.
---

revision.comment: v25: Convert interwiki links [[:en:...]] to {{ill}} templates
revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:益子祇園祭]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:益子祇園祭]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:益子祇園祭]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11581191]] Wikidata name: Mashiko Gion Matsuri [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:20, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:03, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Mashiko Gion Festival''' (益子祇園祭) is a festival held annually from {{ill|July 23|en|July 23|WD=Q2722|lt=July 23}} to {{ill|July 25|en|July 25|WD=Q2720|lt=July 25}} in {{ill|Mashiko, Tochigi|en|Mashiko, Tochigi|WD=Q1346925|lt=Mashiko, Tochigi}}, Tochigi Prefecture. It is the festival of {{ill|Yasaka Shrine|en|Yasaka Shrine|WD=Q692714|lt=Yasaka Shrine (Mashiko)}}, which is a subordinate shrine within the grounds of {{ill|Kashima Shrine (Mashiko)|ja|鹿島神社 (益子町)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashima Shrine (Mashiko)|1=Kashima Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q134891806}}<ref>[https://www.town.mashiko.lg.jp/sp/page/page001983.html "Mashiko Sekai Heritage" No.10｜Yasaka Shrine - Mashiko Town Official Website]</ref><ref name="MashikoJouhouKyoku2012_07_24">{{Cite web | url= http://mashikojk.blog.fc2.com/blog-entry-19.html | title= Mashiko's Summer - Gion Festival Departure Ceremony & Handheld Fireworks Dedication | website= Mashiko Information Bureau | date= 2012-7-24 | accessdate=2024-8-1}}</ref><ref name="TouKo_MashikoGionMaturi_2023">{{Cite instagram|user=tokomashiko |author=Tōko / Mashiko Pottery Gallery |postid=CvOdm-FPlD_ |title=Reiwa 5th Year Mashiko Gion Festival was held from July 23 to 25... |date=2023-07-28|access-date=2024-07-26}}</ref><ref name="Mashiko_no_Omatsuri">[http://www.mashiko-kankou.org/culture/traditional_culture.shtml Mashiko Culture - Traditional Culture | Mashiko Tourism Association]</ref><ref name="MashikoGionMatsuri_MashikoSekenIsan_054">{{Cite web | url= https://www.town.mashiko.lg.jp/sp/page/page002973.html | title= Mashiko Sekai Heritage No.54｜Yasaka Shrine Gion Festival (Mashiko Gion Festival / Tenno Festival) | website= Mashiko Town Official Website | date= | accessdate=2024-7-30}}</ref>.
One of the most famous events is the '''"Omiki Choudai-shiki"''' (御神酒頂戴式), a ritual for passing the town's duty, which is considered one of the '''"Three Great Peculiar Festivals of Kanto"''' and is held on the middle day, {{ill|July 24|en|July 24|WD=Q2721|lt=July 24}}<ref name=MashikoGionMatsuri_MashikoSekenIsan_054/><ref name=TouKo_MashikoGionMaturi_2023/>{{sfn|Kashiwamura Yūji, Festivals of Tochigi|2012|p=106-108}}.
== Overview == The '''Yasaka Shrine''' in Mashiko is a subordinate shrine of {{ill|Kashima Shrine (Mashiko)|ja|鹿島神社 (益子町)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashima Shrine (Mashiko)|1=Kashima Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q134891806}}<ref name=MashikoGionMatsuri_MashikoSekenIsan_054/>. Every year from '''July 23 to July 25''', the Gion Festival ('''formerly called Tenno Festival''') is held<ref name=MashikoJouhouKyoku2012_07_24/><ref name=MashikoGionMatsuri_MashikoSekenIsan_054/>.
The festival is also known as '''"Yasaka Shrine Gion Festival"'''{{sfn|Kashiwamura Yūji, Festivals of Tochigi|2012|p=106-108}}.
The central town districts of Mashiko, such as '''Shinmachi, Tamachi, Uchimachi, Jōnai, and Dōsodo''', take turns being the hosting town. These five towns, along with '''Hanawa District''', participate with '''festival floats''' that parade through Mashiko’s main streets in a grand celebration{{sfn|Mashiko Exploration, Kuramoto Hidekiyo|1992|p=72}}<ref name=MashikoJouhouKyoku2012_07_24/>.
Many participants are not just original Mashiko residents but also '''potters who have moved to Mashiko''', including '''Takauchi Hideyuki''' and '''Susukida Kōji'''{{sfn|Mashiko Exploration, Kuramoto Hidekiyo|1992|p=72-74}}{{sfn|Shimotsuke Newspaper|1984|p=94-95}}<ref name=SusukidaKouji_no_DeshiTachi>{{Cite instagram|user=ito137.3life |author=ito_susukida |postid=BbiaHMtg5kk |title=Father and his seven apprentices... |date=2017-11-18 |access-date=2023-07-19}}</ref>.
== Origin == Around '''Hōei 2 (1705)''', an '''epidemic''' broke out in Mashiko. To pray for its eradication, the '''Gion belief''' (祇園信仰) originating from Kyoto in the Heian period was introduced{{sfn|Michikake Vol.5, Mashiko Town|2015|p=14}}, and people began '''worshiping Gozu Tennō''', the deity of plagues<ref name=MashikoJouhouKyoku2012_07_24/><ref name=TouKo_MashikoGionMaturi_2023/>{{sfn|Kashiwamura Yūji, Festivals of Tochigi|2012|p=106-108}}.
---
=== '''Category & External Links'''=== * [http://www.mashiko-kankou.org/culture/traditional_culture.shtml Mashiko Culture | Traditional Culture | Gion Festival] * '''Mashiko Tourism Association''' * [https://www.town.mashiko.lg.jp/sp/page/page002973.html Mashiko Sekai Heritage No.54 | Yasaka Shrine Gion Festival] * '''Mashiko Town Official Website''' * [https://tabi-mag.jp/mashiko-gion/ Mashiko Gion Festival] * '''Nippon Tabi Magazine'''
{{translated page|ja|益子祇園祭|version=102298556|comment=Imported full ja history}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:益子祇園祭]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:益子祇園祭]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:益子祇園祭]]
'''Mashiko Gion Festival''' (益子祇園祭) is a festival held annually from {{ill|July 23|en|July 23|WD=Q2722|lt=July 23}} to {{ill|July 25|en|July 25|WD=Q2720|lt=July 25}} in {{ill|Mashiko, Tochigi|en|Mashiko, Tochigi|WD=Q1346925|lt=Mashiko, Tochigi}}, Tochigi Prefecture. It is the festival of {{ill|Yasaka Shrine|en|Yasaka Shrine|WD=Q692714|lt=Yasaka Shrine (Mashiko)}}, which is a subordinate shrine within the grounds of {{ill|Kashima Shrine (Mashiko)|ja|鹿島神社 (益子町)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashima Shrine (Mashiko)|1=Kashima Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q134891806}}<ref>[https://www.town.mashiko.lg.jp/sp/page/page001983.html "Mashiko Sekai Heritage" No.10｜Yasaka Shrine - Mashiko Town Official Website]</ref><ref name="MashikoJouhouKyoku2012_07_24">{{Cite web | url= http://mashikojk.blog.fc2.com/blog-entry-19.html | title= Mashiko's Summer - Gion Festival Departure Ceremony & Handheld Fireworks Dedication | website= Mashiko Information Bureau | date= 2012-7-24 | accessdate=2024-8-1}}</ref><ref name="TouKo_MashikoGionMaturi_2023">{{Cite instagram|user=tokomashiko |author=Tōko / Mashiko Pottery Gallery |postid=CvOdm-FPlD_ |title=Reiwa 5th Year Mashiko Gion Festival was held from July 23 to 25... |date=2023-07-28|access-date=2024-07-26}}</ref><ref name="Mashiko_no_Omatsuri">[http://www.mashiko-kankou.org/culture/traditional_culture.shtml Mashiko Culture - Traditional Culture | Mashiko Tourism Association]</ref><ref name="MashikoGionMatsuri_MashikoSekenIsan_054">{{Cite web | url= https://www.town.mashiko.lg.jp/sp/page/page002973.html | title= Mashiko Sekai Heritage No.54｜Yasaka Shrine Gion Festival (Mashiko Gion Festival / Tenno Festival) | website= Mashiko Town Official Website | date= | accessdate=2024-7-30}}</ref>.
One of the most famous events is the '''"Omiki Choudai-shiki"''' (御神酒頂戴式), a ritual for passing the town's duty, which is considered one of the '''"Three Great Peculiar Festivals of Kanto"''' and is held on the middle day, {{ill|July 24|en|July 24|WD=Q2721|lt=July 24}}<ref name=MashikoGionMatsuri_MashikoSekenIsan_054/><ref name=TouKo_MashikoGionMaturi_2023/>{{sfn|Kashiwamura Yūji, Festivals of Tochigi|2012|p=106-108}}.
== Overview == The '''Yasaka Shrine''' in Mashiko is a subordinate shrine of {{ill|Kashima Shrine (Mashiko)|ja|鹿島神社 (益子町)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashima Shrine (Mashiko)|1=Kashima Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q134891806}}<ref name=MashikoGionMatsuri_MashikoSekenIsan_054/>. Every year from '''July 23 to July 25''', the Gion Festival ('''formerly called Tenno Festival''') is held<ref name=MashikoJouhouKyoku2012_07_24/><ref name=MashikoGionMatsuri_MashikoSekenIsan_054/>.
The festival is also known as '''"Yasaka Shrine Gion Festival"'''{{sfn|Kashiwamura Yūji, Festivals of Tochigi|2012|p=106-108}}.
The central town districts of Mashiko, such as '''Shinmachi, Tamachi, Uchimachi, Jōnai, and Dōsodo''', take turns being the hosting town. These five towns, along with '''Hanawa District''', participate with '''festival floats''' that parade through Mashiko’s main streets in a grand celebration{{sfn|Mashiko Exploration, Kuramoto Hidekiyo|1992|p=72}}<ref name=MashikoJouhouKyoku2012_07_24/>.
Many participants are not just original Mashiko residents but also '''potters who have moved to Mashiko''', including '''Takauchi Hideyuki''' and '''Susukida Kōji'''{{sfn|Mashiko Exploration, Kuramoto Hidekiyo|1992|p=72-74}}{{sfn|Shimotsuke Newspaper|1984|p=94-95}}<ref name=SusukidaKouji_no_DeshiTachi>{{Cite instagram|user=ito137.3life |author=ito_susukida |postid=BbiaHMtg5kk |title=Father and his seven apprentices... |date=2017-11-18 |access-date=2023-07-19}}</ref>.
== Origin == Around '''Hōei 2 (1705)''', an '''epidemic''' broke out in Mashiko. To pray for its eradication, the '''Gion belief''' (祇園信仰) originating from Kyoto in the Heian period was introduced{{sfn|Michikake Vol.5, Mashiko Town|2015|p=14}}, and people began '''worshiping Gozu Tennō''', the deity of plagues<ref name=MashikoJouhouKyoku2012_07_24/><ref name=TouKo_MashikoGionMaturi_2023/>{{sfn|Kashiwamura Yūji, Festivals of Tochigi|2012|p=106-108}}.
---
=== '''Category & External Links'''=== * [http://www.mashiko-kankou.org/culture/traditional_culture.shtml Mashiko Culture | Traditional Culture | Gion Festival] * '''Mashiko Tourism Association''' * [https://www.town.mashiko.lg.jp/sp/page/page002973.html Mashiko Sekai Heritage No.54 | Yasaka Shrine Gion Festival] * '''Mashiko Town Official Website''' * [https://tabi-mag.jp/mashiko-gion/ Mashiko Gion Festival] * '''Nippon Tabi Magazine'''
{{translated page|ja|益子祇園祭|version=102298556|comment=Imported full ja history}}

'''Mashiko Gion Festival''' (益子祇園祭) is a festival held annually from {{ill|July 23|en|July 23|qid=Q2722|lt=July 23|1=July 23}} to {{ill|July 25|en|July 25|qid=Q2720|lt=July 25|1=July 25}} in {{ill|Mashiko, Tochigi|en|Mashiko, Tochigi|qid=Q1346925|lt=Mashiko, Tochigi|1=Mashiko, Tochigi}}, Tochigi Prefecture. It is the festival of {{ill|Yasaka Shrine|en|Yasaka Shrine|qid=Q692714|lt=Yasaka Shrine (Mashiko)|1=Yasaka Shrine}}, which is a subordinate shrine within the grounds of {{ill|Kashima Shrine (Mashiko)|ja|鹿島神社 (益子町)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashima Shrine (Mashiko)|1=Kashima Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q134891806}}<ref>[https://www.town.mashiko.lg.jp/sp/page/page001983.html "Mashiko Sekai Heritage" No.10｜Yasaka Shrine - Mashiko Town Official Website]</ref><ref name="MashikoJouhouKyoku2012_07_24">{{Cite web | url= http://mashikojk.blog.fc2.com/blog-entry-19.html | title= Mashiko's Summer - Gion Festival Departure Ceremony & Handheld Fireworks Dedication | website= Mashiko Information Bureau | date= 2012-7-24 | accessdate=2024-8-1}}</ref><ref name="TouKo_MashikoGionMaturi_2023">{{Cite instagram|user=tokomashiko |author=Tōko / Mashiko Pottery Gallery |postid=CvOdm-FPlD_ |title=Reiwa 5th Year Mashiko Gion Festival was held from July 23 to 25... |date=2023-07-28|access-date=2024-07-26}}</ref><ref name="Mashiko_no_Omatsuri">[http://www.mashiko-kankou.org/culture/traditional_culture.shtml Mashiko Culture - Traditional Culture | Mashiko Tourism Association]</ref><ref name="MashikoGionMatsuri_MashikoSekenIsan_054">{{Cite web | url= https://www.town.mashiko.lg.jp/sp/page/page002973.html | title= Mashiko Sekai Heritage No.54｜Yasaka Shrine Gion Festival (Mashiko Gion Festival / Tenno Festival) | website= Mashiko Town Official Website | date= | accessdate=2024-7-30}}</ref>.
One of the most famous events is the '''"Omiki Choudai-shiki"''' (御神酒頂戴式), a ritual for passing the town's duty, which is considered one of the '''"Three Great Peculiar Festivals of Kanto"''' and is held on the middle day, {{ill|July 24|en|July 24|qid=Q2721|lt=July 24|1=July 24}}<ref name=MashikoGionMatsuri_MashikoSekenIsan_054/><ref name=TouKo_MashikoGionMaturi_2023/>{{sfn|Kashiwamura Yūji, Festivals of Tochigi|2012|p=106-108}}.
== Overview == The '''Yasaka Shrine''' in Mashiko is a subordinate shrine of {{ill|Kashima Shrine (Mashiko)|ja|鹿島神社 (益子町)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashima Shrine (Mashiko)|1=Kashima Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q134891806}}<ref name=MashikoGionMatsuri_MashikoSekenIsan_054/>. Every year from '''July 23 to July 25''', the Gion Festival ('''formerly called Tenno Festival''') is held<ref name=MashikoJouhouKyoku2012_07_24/><ref name=MashikoGionMatsuri_MashikoSekenIsan_054/>.
The festival is also known as '''"Yasaka Shrine Gion Festival"'''{{sfn|Kashiwamura Yūji, Festivals of Tochigi|2012|p=106-108}}.
The central town districts of Mashiko, such as '''Shinmachi, Tamachi, Uchimachi, Jōnai, and Dōsodo''', take turns being the hosting town. These five towns, along with '''Hanawa District''', participate with '''festival floats''' that parade through Mashiko’s main streets in a grand celebration{{sfn|Mashiko Exploration, Kuramoto Hidekiyo|1992|p=72}}<ref name=MashikoJouhouKyoku2012_07_24/>.
Many participants are not just original Mashiko residents but also '''potters who have moved to Mashiko''', including '''Takauchi Hideyuki''' and '''Susukida Kōji'''{{sfn|Mashiko Exploration, Kuramoto Hidekiyo|1992|p=72-74}}{{sfn|Shimotsuke Newspaper|1984|p=94-95}}<ref name=SusukidaKouji_no_DeshiTachi>{{Cite instagram|user=ito137.3life |author=ito_susukida |postid=BbiaHMtg5kk |title=Father and his seven apprentices... |date=2017-11-18 |access-date=2023-07-19}}</ref>.
== Origin == Around '''Hōei 2 (1705)''', an '''epidemic''' broke out in Mashiko. To pray for its eradication, the '''Gion belief''' (祇園信仰) originating from Kyoto in the Heian period was introduced{{sfn|Michikake Vol.5, Mashiko Town|2015|p=14}}, and people began '''worshiping Gozu Tennō''', the deity of plagues<ref name=MashikoJouhouKyoku2012_07_24/><ref name=TouKo_MashikoGionMaturi_2023/>{{sfn|Kashiwamura Yūji, Festivals of Tochigi|2012|p=106-108}}.
---
